25.text: Your Lord is the Best Knower of that which is in your souls; if ye have been righteous, then He is unto thee Oftreturning, Forgiving.

25.author: Hilali & Khan
25.text: Your Lord knows best what is in your inner-selves. If you are righteous, then, verily, He is Ever Most Forgiving to those who turn unto Him again and again in obedience, and in repentance.

25.author: Itani
25.text: Your Lord knows best what is in your minds. If you are righteous—He is Forgiving to the obedient.

25.author: Maududi
25.text: Your Lord is best aware of what is in your hearts. If you are righteous, He will indeed forgive those who relent and revert (to serving Allah).

25.author: Mubarakpuri
25.text: Your Lord knows best what is in your souls. If you are righteous, then, verily, He is Ever Most Forgiving to those who turn to Him in repentance.

25.author: Pickthall
25.text: Your Lord is Best Aware of what is in your minds. If ye are righteous, then lo! He was ever Forgiving unto those who turn (unto Him).

25.author: Qarai
25.text: Your Lord knows best what is in your hearts. Should you be righteous, He is indeed most forgiving toward penitents.

25.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
25.text: Your Lord knows very well what is in your hearts. If you are good, He is forgiving to those who are penitent.

25.author: Saheeh International
25.text: Your Lord is most knowing of what is within yourselves. If you should be righteous [in intention] - then indeed He is ever, to the often returning [to Him], Forgiving.

25.author: Sarwar
25.text: Your Lord knows what is in your souls. If you would be righteous, know that He is All-forgiving to those who turn to Him in repentance.

25.author: Shakir
25.text: Your Lord knows best what is in your minds; if you are good, then He is surely Forgiving to those who turn (to Him) frequently.

25.author: Wahiduddin Khan
25.text: Your Lord knows best what is in your hearts; if you are righteous, He is most forgiving to those who constantly turn to Him.

25.author: Yusuf Ali
25.text: Your Lord knoweth best what is in your hearts: If ye do deeds of righteousness, verily He is Most Forgiving to those who turn to Him again and again (in true penitence).

25.author: Transliteration
25.text: Rabbukum aAAlamu bima fee nufoosikum in takoonoo saliheena fainnahu kana lilawwabeena ghafooran

26.author: Ahmed Ali
26.text: So give to your relatives what is their due, and to those who are needy, and the wayfarers; and do not dissipate (your wealth) extravagantly.

26.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
26.text: And give the relatives their rights, and to the needy, and to the traveller; and do not waste needlessly.

26.author: Arberry
26.text: And give the kinsman his right, and the needy, and the traveller; and never squander;

26.author: Daryabadi
26.text: And give thou to the kinsman his due, and also unto the needy and the wayfarer; and squander not in squandering.

26.author: Hilali & Khan
26.text: And give to the kindred his due and to the Miskin (poor) and to the wayfarer. But spend not wastefully (your wealth) in the manner of a spendthrift. [Tafsir. At-Tabari, Vol. 10, Page 158 (Verse 9:60)].

26.author: Itani
26.text: And give the relative his rights, and the poor, and the wayfarer, and do not squander wastefully.

26.author: Maududi
26.text: (iii) Give to the near of kin his due, and also to the needy and the wayfarer. (iv) Do not squander your wealth wastefully,

26.author: Mubarakpuri
26.text: And give to the kinsman his due, and to the Miskin (poor), and to the wayfarer. But spend not wastefully (your wealth) in the manner of a spendthrift.

26.author: Pickthall
26.text: Give the kinsman his due, and the needy, and the wayfarer, and squander not (thy wealth) in wantonness.

26.author: Qarai
26.text: Give the relatives their [due] right, and the needy and the traveller [as well], but do not squander wastefully.

26.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
26.text: Give to the near of kin, the needy and the destitute traveler their rights and do not squander,

26.author: Saheeh International
26.text: And give the relative his right, and [also] the poor and the traveler, and do not spend wastefully.

26.author: Sarwar
26.text: Give the relatives, the destitute and those who when on a journey have become needy, their dues.

26.author: Shakir
26.text: And give to the near of kin his due and (to) the needy and the wayfarer, and do not squander wastefully.

26.author: Wahiduddin Khan
26.text: Give to your relatives their due, and also to the needy and the wayfarer. Yet do not spend extravagantly;

26.author: Yusuf Ali
26.text: And render to the kindred their due rights, as (also) to those in want, and to the wayfarer: But squander not (your wealth) in the manner of a spendthrift.

26.author: Transliteration
26.text: Waati tha alqurba haqqahu waalmiskeena waibna alssabeeli wala tubaththir tabtheeran

27.author: Ahmed Ali
27.text: Those who dissipate (their wealth) are the brethren of the devils, and the Devil was ungrateful to his Lord.

27.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
27.text: Indeed those who needlessly waste are brothers of the devils; and the devil is very ungrateful to his Lord.

27.author: Arberry
27.text: the squanderers are brothers of Satan, and Satan is unthankful to his Lord.

27.author: Daryabadi
27.text: Verily the squanderers are ever the brethren of the Satans, and the satan is ever unto his Lord ungrateful.

27.author: Hilali & Khan
27.text: Verily, spendthrifts are brothers of the Shayatin (devils), and the Shaitan (Devil - Satan) is ever ungrateful to his Lord.

27.author: Itani
27.text: The extravagant are brethren of the devils, and the devil is ever ungrateful to his Lord.

27.author: Maududi
27.text: for those who squander wastefully are Satan's brothers, and Satan is ever ungrateful to his Lord.

27.author: Mubarakpuri
27.text: Verily, the spendthrifts are brothers of the Shayatin (devils), and the Shaytan is ever ungrateful to his Lord.

27.author: Pickthall
27.text: Lo! the squanderers were ever brothers of the devils, and the devil was ever an ingrate to his Lord.

27.author: Qarai
27.text: Indeed the wasteful are brothers of satans, and Satan is ungrateful to his Lord.

27.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
27.text: for the wasteful are the brothers of satan; and satan is ungrateful to his Lord.

27.author: Saheeh International
27.text: Indeed, the wasteful are brothers of the devils, and ever has Satan been to his Lord ungrateful.

27.author: Sarwar
27.text: Do not be a wasteful spender. Squanderers are the brothers of satan. Satan was faithless to his Lord.

27.author: Shakir
27.text: Surely the squanderers are the fellows of the Shaitans and the Shaitan is ever ungrateful to his Lord.

27.author: Wahiduddin Khan
27.text: spendthrifts are the brothers of Satan, and Satan is ever ungrateful to his Lord --

27.author: Yusuf Ali
27.text: Verily spendthrifts are brothers of the Evil Ones; and the Evil One is to his Lord (himself) ungrateful.

27.author: Transliteration
27.text: Inna almubaththireena kanoo ikhwana alshshayateeni wakana alshshaytanu lirabbihi kafooran

28.author: Ahmed Ali
28.text: If you neglect (your parents) while seeking the bounty of your Lord, of which you are hopeful, speak to them softly.

28.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
28.text: And if you turn away from these*, expecting the mercy of your Lord, for which you hope, then speak to them an easy word. (* The companions of the Holy Prophet, who sought his assistance.)

28.author: Arberry
28.text: But if thou turnest from them, seeking mercy from thy Lord that thou hopest for, then speak unto them gentle words.

28.author: Daryabadi
28.text: And if thou turnest away from them awaiting a mercy from thy Lord which thou hopest, then speak unto them a gentle speech.

28.author: Hilali & Khan
28.text: And if you (O Muhammad SAW) turn away from them (kindred, poor, wayfarer, etc. whom We have ordered you to give their rights, but if you have no money at the time they ask you for it) and you are awaiting a mercy from your Lord for which you hope, then, speak unto them a soft kind word (i.e. Allah will give me and I shall give you).

28.author: Itani
28.text: But if you turn away from them, seeking mercy from your Lord which you hope for, then say to them words of comfort.

28.author: Maududi
28.text: (v) And when you must turn away from them - (that is, from the destitute, the near of kin, the needy, and the wayfarer) - in pursuit of God's Mercy which you expect to receive, then speak to them kindly.

28.author: Mubarakpuri
28.text: And if you turn away from them and you are awaiting a mercy from your Lord for which you hope, then, speak unto them a soft, kind word.

28.author: Pickthall
28.text: But if thou turn away from them, seeking mercy from thy Lord, for which thou hopest, then speak unto them a reasonable word.

28.author: Qarai
28.text: And if you have to hold off from [assisting] them [for now], seeking your Lord’s mercy which you expect [in the future], speak to them gentle words.

28.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
28.text: But if you turn away from them to seek the Mercy of your Lord, hoping to attain it, then speak to them with gentle words.

28.author: Saheeh International
28.text: And if you [must] turn away from the needy awaiting mercy from your Lord which you expect, then speak to them a gentle word.

28.author: Sarwar
28.text: If you are not able to assist them, at least speak to them in a kind manner.

28.author: Shakir
28.text: And if you turn away from them to seek mercy from your Lord, which you hope for, speak to them a gentle word.

28.author: Wahiduddin Khan
28.text: but if, while waiting for your Lord's bounty which you are expecting, you turn them down, then at least speak to them kindly.

28.author: Yusuf Ali
28.text: And even if thou hast to turn away from them in pursuit of the Mercy from thy Lord which thou dost expect, yet speak to them a word of easy kindness.

28.author: Transliteration
28.text: Waimma tuAAridanna AAanhumu ibtighaa rahmatin min rabbika tarjooha faqul lahum qawlan maysooran

29.author: Ahmed Ali
29.text: Do not be niggardly, nor extravagant that you may later feel reprehensive and constrained.

29.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
29.text: And do not keep your hand tied to your neck nor open it completely, lest you remain seated – reproached, weary.

29.author: Arberry
29.text: And keep not thy hand chained to thy neck, nor outspread it widespread altogether, or thou wilt sit reproached and denuded.

29.author: Daryabadi
29.text: And let not thine hand be chained to thy neck, nor stretch it forth to the utmost stretching, lest thou sit down reproached, impoverished.

29.author: Hilali & Khan
29.text: And let not your hand be tied (like a miser) to your neck, nor stretch it forth to its utmost reach (like a spendthrift), so that you become blameworthy and in severe poverty.

29.author: Itani
29.text: And do not keep your hand tied to your neck, nor spread it out fully, lest you end up liable and regretful.

29.author: Maududi
29.text: (vi) Do not keep your hand fastened to your neck nor outspread it, altogether outspread, for you will be left sitting rebuked, destitute.

29.author: Mubarakpuri
29.text: And let not your hand be tied (like a miser) to your neck, nor overextend it (like a spendthrift), so that you become blameworthy and in severe poverty.

29.author: Pickthall
29.text: And let not thy hand be chained to thy neck nor open it with a complete opening, lest thou sit down rebuked, denuded.

29.author: Qarai
29.text: Do not keep your hand chained to your neck, nor open it altogether, or you will sit blameworthy and regretful.

29.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
29.text: And do not keep your hand chained to your neck (when spending), nor open it completely, so that you will sit blamed and destitute.

29.author: Saheeh International
29.text: And do not make your hand [as] chained to your neck or extend it completely and [thereby] become blamed and insolvent.

29.author: Sarwar
29.text: Do not be stingy nor over generous lest you become empty handed and bankrupt.

29.author: Shakir
29.text: And do not make your hand to be shackled to your neck nor stretch it forth to the utmost (limit) of its stretching forth, lest you should (afterwards) sit down blamed, stripped off.

29.author: Wahiduddin Khan
29.text: Be neither miserly, nor so open-handed that you suffer reproach and become destitute.

29.author: Yusuf Ali
29.text: Make not thy hand tied (like a niggard's) to thy neck, nor stretch it forth to its utmost reach, so that thou become blameworthy and destitute.

29.author: Transliteration
29.text: Wala tajAAal yadaka maghloolatan ila AAunuqika wala tabsutha kulla albasti fataqAAuda malooman mahsooran

30.author: Ahmed Ali
30.text: Certainly your Lord provides with open hands whosoever He will, but according to capacity, for He knows and watches His creatures.

30.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
30.text: Indeed your Lord eases the livelihood and restricts it, for whomever He wills; He Well Knows, Beholds His bondmen.

30.author: Arberry
30.text: Surely thy Lord outspreads and straitens His provision unto whom He will; surely He is aware of and sees His servants.

30.author: Daryabadi
30.text: Verily thy Lord extendeth the provision for whomsoever He will and He measureth it out; verily He is in respect of His bondmen Aware, Beholder.

30.author: Hilali & Khan
30.text: Truly, your Lord enlarges the provision for whom He wills and straitens (for whom He wills). Verily, He is Ever All-Knower, All-Seer of His slaves.

30.author: Itani
30.text: Your Lord expands the provision for whomever He wills, and restricts it. He is fully Informed, Observant of His servants.

30.author: Maududi
30.text: Certainly Your Lord makes plentiful the provision of whomsoever He wills and straitens it for whomsoever He wills. He is well-aware and is fully observant of all that relates to His servants.

30.author: Mubarakpuri
30.text: Truly, your Lord expands the provision for whom He wills and straitens (for whom He wills). Verily, He is Ever All-Knower, All-Seer of His servants.

30.author: Pickthall
30.text: Lo! thy Lord enlargeth the provision for whom He will, and straiteneth (it for whom He will). Lo, He was ever Knower, Seer of His slaves.

30.author: Qarai
30.text: Indeed your Lord expands the provision for whomever He wishes, and tightens it. Indeed, He is well aware of His servants and a keen observer.

30.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
30.text: Your Lord gives to whom He will His provisions both abundantly and sparingly. He is aware and sees His worshipers.

30.author: Saheeh International
30.text: Indeed, your Lord extends provision for whom He wills and restricts [it]. Indeed He is ever, concerning His servants, Acquainted and Seeing.

30.author: Sarwar
30.text: Your Lord increases and determines the sustenance of whomever He wants. He is Well Aware and watches over His servants.

30.author: Shakir
30.text: Surely your Lord makes plentiful the means of subsistence for whom He pleases and He straitens (them); surely He is ever Aware of, Seeing, His servants.

30.author: Wahiduddin Khan
30.text: Your Lord gives abundantly to whom He will and sparingly to whom He pleases. He is informed and observant about His servants.

30.author: Yusuf Ali
30.text: Verily thy Lord doth provide sustenance in abundance for whom He pleaseth, and He provideth in a just measure. For He doth know and regard all His servants.

30.author: Transliteration
30.text: Inna rabbaka yabsutu alrrizqa liman yashao wayaqdiru innahu kana biAAibadihi khabeeran baseeran

31.author: Ahmed Ali
31.text: Do not abandon your children out of fear of poverty. We will provide for them and for you. Killing them is certainly a great wrong.

31.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
31.text: And do not kill your children, fearing poverty; We shall provide sustenance to them as well as to you; indeed killing them is a great mistake.

31.author: Arberry
31.text: And slay not your children for fear of poverty; We will provide for you and them; surely the slaying of them is a grievous sin.

31.author: Daryabadi
31.text: And saly not your offspring for fear of want; We provide for them and for yourselves; verily their slaying is a great crime.

31.author: Hilali & Khan
31.text: And kill not your children for fear of poverty. We provide for them and for you. Surely, the killing of them is a great sin.

31.author: Itani
31.text: And do not kill your children for fear of poverty. We provide for them, and for you. Killing them is a grave sin.

31.author: Maududi
31.text: (vii) Do not kill your children for fear of want. We will provide for them and for you. Surely killing them is a great sin.

31.author: Mubarakpuri
31.text: And kill not your children for fear of poverty. We shall provide for them as well as for you. Surely, the killing of them is a great sin.

31.author: Pickthall
31.text: Slay not your children, fearing a fall to poverty, We shall provide for them and for you. Lo! the slaying of them is great sin.

31.author: Qarai
31.text: Do not kill your children for the fear of penury: We will provide for them and for you. Killing them is indeed a great iniquity.

31.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
31.text: Do not kill your children because you fear poverty. We will provide for you and them. Killing them is a great sin.

31.author: Saheeh International
31.text: And do not kill your children for fear of poverty. We provide for them and for you. Indeed, their killing is ever a great sin.

31.author: Sarwar
31.text: Do not kill your children for fear of poverty. We will give sustenance to all of you. To kill them is certainly a great sin.

31.author: Shakir
31.text: And do not kill your children for fear of poverty; We give them sustenance and yourselves (too); surely to kill them is a great wrong.

31.author: Wahiduddin Khan
31.text: You shall not kill your offspring for fear of want. It is We who provide for them, and for you. Indeed, killing them is a great sin.

31.author: Yusuf Ali
31.text: Kill not your children for fear of want: We shall provide sustenance for them as well as for you. Verily the killing of them is a great sin.

31.author: Transliteration
31.text: Wala taqtuloo awladakum khashyata imlaqin nahnu narzuquhum waiyyakum inna qatlahum kana khitan kabeeran

32.author: Ahmed Ali
32.text: And do not go near fornication, as it is immoral and an evil way.

32.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
32.text: And do not approach adultery – it is indeed a shameful deed; and a very evil way.

32.author: Arberry
32.text: And approach not fornication; surely it is an indecency, and evil as a way.

32.author: Daryabadi
32.text: And approach not adultery; verily it is ever an abomination and vile as a pathway.

32.author: Hilali & Khan
32.text: And come not near to the unlawful sexual intercourse. Verily, it is a Fahishah [i.e. anything that transgresses its limits (a great sin)], and an evil way (that leads one to Hell unless Allah forgives him).

32.author: Itani
32.text: And do not come near adultery. It is immoral, and an evil way.

32.author: Maududi
32.text: (viii) Do not even approach fornication for it is an outrageous act, and an evil way.

32.author: Mubarakpuri
32.text: And come not near to unlawful sex. Verily, it is Fahishah (immoral sin) and an evil way.

32.author: Pickthall
32.text: And come not near unto adultery. Lo! it is an abomination and an evil way.

32.author: Qarai
32.text: Do not approach fornication. It is indeed an indecency and an evil way.

32.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
32.text: Do not draw near to fornication, for it is an indecency, and its way is evil.

32.author: Saheeh International
32.text: And do not approach unlawful sexual intercourse. Indeed, it is ever an immorality and is evil as a way.

32.author: Sarwar
32.text: Do not even approach adultery. It is indecent and an evil act.

32.author: Shakir
32.text: And go not nigh to fornication; surely it is an indecency and an evil way.

32.author: Wahiduddin Khan
32.text: Do not commit adultery, for it is an indecent thing and an evil course.

32.author: Yusuf Ali
32.text: Nor come nigh to adultery: for it is a shameful (deed) and an evil, opening the road (to other evils).

32.author: Transliteration
32.text: Wala taqraboo alzzina innahu kana fahishatan wasaa sabeelan

33.author: Ahmed Ali
33.text: And do not take a life, which God has forbidden, except in a just cause. We have given the right (of redress) to the heir of the person who is killed, but he should not exceed the limits (of justice) by slaying (the killer), for he will be judged (by the same law).

33.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
33.text: And do not wrongfully kill any living being which Allah has forbidden; and for whoever is slain wrongfully, We have given the authority to his heir, so he should not cross limits in slaying; he will surely be helped.

33.author: Arberry
33.text: And slay not the soul God has forbidden, except by right. Whosoever is slain unjustly, We have appointed to his next-of-kin authority; but let him not exceed in slaying; he shall be helped.

33.author: Daryabadi
33.text: And slay not anyone whom Allah hath forbidden except for justification; and whosoever is slain wrongfully, We have surely given his next-of-kin authority so let him not be extravagant in slaying; verily he is ever succoured.

33.author: Hilali & Khan
33.text: And do not kill anyone which Allah has forbidden, except for a just cause. And whoever is killed (intentionally with hostility and oppression and not by mistake), We have given his heir the authority [(to demand Qisas, Law of Equality in punishment or to forgive, or to take Diya (blood money)]. But let him not exceed limits in the matter of taking life (i.e. he should not kill except the killer only). Verily, he is helped (by the Islamic law).

33.author: Itani
33.text: And do not kill the soul which God has made sacred, except in the course of justice. If someone is killed unjustly, We have given his next of kin certain authority. But he should not be excessive in killing, for he will be supported.

33.author: Maududi
33.text: (ix) Do not kill any person whom Allah has forbidden to kill, except with right. We have granted the heir of him who has been wrongfully killed the authority to (claim retribution); so let him not exceed in slaying. He shall be helped.

33.author: Mubarakpuri
33.text: And do not kill anyone whose killing Allah has forbidden, except for a just cause. And whoever is killed wrongfully (not by mistake), We have given his heir the authority. But let him not exceed limits in the matter of taking life. Verily, he is helped.

33.author: Pickthall
33.text: And slay not the life which Allah hath forbidden save with right. Whoso is slain wrongfully, We have given power unto his heir, but let him not commit excess in slaying. Lo! he will be helped.

33.author: Qarai
33.text: Do not kill a soul [whose life] Allah has made inviolable, except with due cause, and whoever is killed wrongfully, We have certainly given his heir an authority. But let him not commit any excess in killing [the murderer], for he has been assisted [by law].

33.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
33.text: Do not kill the soul whom Allah has forbidden except by right. If he is slain unjustly, We have given his heir authority. But let him not exceed the limit in slaying, for he will be helped.

33.author: Saheeh International
33.text: And do not kill the soul which Allah has forbidden, except by right. And whoever is killed unjustly - We have given his heir authority, but let him not exceed limits in [the matter of] taking life. Indeed, he has been supported [by the law].

33.author: Sarwar
33.text: Do not kill a respectable soul without a just cause. If anyone is wrongfully killed, we have given the heirs of that person the right (to demand satisfaction or to forgive). He must not exceed the law in having vengeance; his victim shall also be assisted (by law).

33.author: Shakir
33.text: And do not kill any one whom Allah has forbidden, except for a just cause, and whoever is slain unjustly, We have indeed given to his heir authority, so let him not exceed the just limits in slaying; surely he is aided.

33.author: Wahiduddin Khan
33.text: Do not take life which God has made inviolate -- except by right. If anyone is killed wrongfully, We have given authority to his heirs to demand retribution, but let them not transgress the prescribed limits in exacting retribution; for then he will be assisted [by the law].

33.author: Yusuf Ali
33.text: Nor take life - which Allah has made sacred - except for just cause. And if anyone is slain wrongfully, we have given his heir authority (to demand qisas or to forgive): but let him not exceed bounds in the matter of taking life; for he is helped (by the Law).

33.author: Transliteration
33.text: Wala taqtuloo alnnafsa allatee harrama Allahu illa bialhaqqi waman qutila mathlooman faqad jaAAalna liwaliyyihi sultanan fala yusrif fee alqatli innahu kana mansooran

34.author: Ahmed Ali
34.text: And do not touch the property of the orphans except for bettering it, until they come of age; and fulfil the promise made: You will surely be questioned about the promise.

34.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
34.text: And do not approach the wealth of the orphan except in the best possible way, till he reaches adulthood; and fulfil the promise; indeed the promise will be asked about.

34.author: Arberry
34.text: And do not approach the property of the orphan save in the fairest manner, until he is of age. And fulfil the covenant; surely the covenant shall be questioned of.

34.author: Daryabadi
34.text: And approach not the substance of an'orphan save with that which is best, until he attaineth his age of strength. And fulfil the covenant; verily the covenant shall be asked about.

34.author: Hilali & Khan
34.text: And come not near to the orphan's property except to improve it, until he attains the age of full strength. And fulfil (every) covenant. Verily! the covenant, will be questioned about.

34.author: Itani
34.text: And do not go near the orphan’s property, except with the best of intentions, until he has reached his maturity. And honor your pledge, because the pledge involves responsibility.

34.author: Maududi
34.text: (x) And do not even go near the property of the orphan - except that it be in the best manner - till he attains his maturity. (xi) And fulfil the covenant, for you will be called to account regarding the covenant.

34.author: Mubarakpuri
34.text: And come not near to the orphan's property except to improve it, until he attains the age of full strength. And fulfill (every) covenant. Verily, the covenant will be questioned about.

34.author: Pickthall
34.text: Come not near the wealth of the orphan save with that which is better till he come to strength; and keep the covenant. Lo! of the covenant it will be asked.

34.author: Qarai
34.text: Do not approach the orphan’s property except in the best manner, until he comes of age. Fulfill the covenants; indeed all covenants are accountable.

34.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
34.text: Do not draw near the wealth of the orphan except in the best manner, until he reaches maturity. And keep your promise. Surely, the promise will be questioned.

34.author: Saheeh International
34.text: And do not approach the property of an orphan, except in the way that is best, until he reaches maturity. And fulfill [every] commitment. Indeed, the commitment is ever [that about which one will be] questioned.

34.author: Sarwar
34.text: Do not get close to the property of the orphans (unless it is for a good reason) until they are mature and strong. Keep your promise; you will be questioned about it.

34.author: Shakir
34.text: And draw not near to the property of the orphan except in a goodly way till he attains his maturity and fulfill the promise; surely (every) promise shall be questioned about.

34.author: Wahiduddin Khan
34.text: Do not go near the orphans' property, except with the best of intentions, until they reach maturity. Keep your promises; you will be called to account for every promise which you have made!

34.author: Yusuf Ali
34.text: Come not nigh to the orphan's property except to improve it, until he attains the age of full strength; and fulfil (every) engagement, for (every) engagement will be enquired into (on the Day of Reckoning).

34.author: Transliteration
34.text: Wala taqraboo mala alyateemi illa biallatee hiya ahsanu hatta yablugha ashuddahu waawfoo bialAAahdi inna alAAahda kana masoolan

35.author: Ahmed Ali
35.text: Give full measure when you are measuring, and weigh on a balanced scale. This is better, and excellent its consequence.

35.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
35.text: And correctly measure when you measure, and weigh correctly with the scales; this is better, and has a better outcome.

35.author: Arberry
35.text: And fill up the measure when you measure, and weigh with the straight balance; that is better and fairer in the issue.

35.author: Daryabadi
35.text: And give full measures when ye measure and weigh with an even balance that is good, and the best interpretation.

35.author: Hilali & Khan
35.text: And give full measure when you measure, and weigh with a balance that is straight. That is good (advantageous) and better in the end.

35.author: Itani
35.text: And give full measure when you measure, and weigh with accurate scales. That is fair, and the best determination.

35.author: Maududi
35.text: (xii) Give full measure when you measure, and weigh with even scales. That is fair and better in consequence.

35.author: Mubarakpuri
35.text: And give full measure when you measure, and weigh with a balance that is straight. That is good (advantageous) and better in the end.

35.author: Pickthall
35.text: Fill the measure when ye measure, and weigh with a right balance; that is meet, and better in the end.

35.author: Qarai
35.text: When you measure, observe fully the measure, [and] weigh with an even balance. That is better and more favourable with respect to the outcome [in the Hereafter].

35.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
35.text: Give full measure when you measure, and weigh with even scales. That is better, and fairer in the end.

35.author: Saheeh International
35.text: And give full measure when you measure, and weigh with an even balance. That is the best [way] and best in result.

35.author: Sarwar
35.text: While weighing, use proper measurements in the exchange of your property. This is fair and will be better in the end.

35.author: Shakir
35.text: And give full measure when you measure out, and weigh with a true balance; this is fair and better in the end.

35.author: Wahiduddin Khan
35.text: Give full measure, when you measure, and weigh with accurate scales. That is fair, and better in the end.

35.author: Yusuf Ali
35.text: Give full measure when ye measure, and weigh with a balance that is straight: that is the most fitting and the most advantageous in the final determination.

35.author: Transliteration
35.text: Waawfoo alkayla itha kiltum wazinoo bialqistasi almustaqeemi thalika khayrun waahsanu taweelan

36.author: Ahmed Ali
36.text: Do not follow that of which you have no knowledge. Verily the ear, the eye, the heart, each will be questioned.

36.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
36.text: And do not pursue the matter you do not have the knowledge of; indeed the ear, and the eye, and the heart – each of these will be questioned.

36.author: Arberry
36.text: And pursue not that thou hast no knowledge of; the hearing, the sight, the heart -- all of those shall be questioned of.

36.author: Daryabadi
36.text: And go not thou after that where of thou hast no knowledge verily the hearing and the sight and the heart, each of these shall be asked about.

36.author: Hilali & Khan
36.text: And follow not (O man i.e., say not, or do not or witness not, etc.) that of which you have no knowledge (e.g. one's saying: "I have seen," while in fact he has not seen, or "I have heard," while he has not heard). Verily! The hearing, and the sight, and the heart, of each of those you will be questioned (by Allah).

36.author: Itani
36.text: And do not occupy yourself with what you have no knowledge of. The hearing, and the sight, and the brains—all these will be questioned.

36.author: Maududi
36.text: (xiii) Do not follow that of which you have no knowledge. Surely the hearing, the sight, the heart - each of these shall be called to account.

36.author: Mubarakpuri
36.text: And follow not that of which you have no knowledge. Verily, the hearing, and the sight, and the heart of each of those ones will be questioned (by Allah).

36.author: Pickthall
36.text: (O man), follow not that whereof thou hast no knowledge. Lo! the hearing and the sight and the heart - of each of these it will be asked.

36.author: Qarai
36.text: Do not pursue that of which you have no knowledge. Indeed hearing, eyesight, and the heart—all of these are accountable.

36.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
36.text: Do not follow what you do not know. The hearing, sight and heart about all these you shall be questioned.

36.author: Saheeh International
36.text: And do not pursue that of which you have no knowledge. Indeed, the hearing, the sight and the heart - about all those [one] will be questioned.

36.author: Sarwar
36.text: Do not follow what you do not know; the ears, eyes, and hearts will all be held responsible for their deeds.

36.author: Shakir
36.text: And follow not that of which you have not the knowledge; surely the hearing and the sight and the heart, all of these, shall be questioned about that.

36.author: Wahiduddin Khan
36.text: Do not follow what you do not know; for the ear and the eye and the heart shall all be called to account.

36.author: Yusuf Ali
36.text: And pursue not that of which thou hast no knowledge; for every act of hearing, or of seeing or of (feeling in) the heart will be enquired into (on the Day of Reckoning).

36.author: Transliteration
36.text: Wala taqfu ma laysa laka bihi AAilmun inna alssamAAa waalbasara waalfuada kullu olaika kana AAanhu masoolan

37.author: Ahmed Ali
37.text: And do not strut about the land with insolence: Surely you cannot cleave the earth, nor attain the height of mountains in stature.

37.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
37.text: And do not walk haughtily on the earth; you can never split the earth, nor be as high as the hills.

37.author: Arberry
37.text: And walk not in the earth exultantly; certainly thou wilt never tear the earth open, nor attain the mountains in height.

37.author: Daryabadi
37.text: And walk thou not on the earth struttingly; verily thou wilt by no means rend the earth, nor canst thou attain to the mountains in stature.

37.author: Hilali & Khan
37.text: And walk not on the earth with conceit and arrogance. Verily, you can neither rend nor penetrate the earth, nor can you attain a stature like the mountains in height.

37.author: Itani
37.text: And do not walk proudly on earth. You can neither pierce the earth, nor can you match the mountains in height.

37.author: Maududi
37.text: (xiv) Do not strut about in the land arrogantly. Surely you cannot cleave the earth, nor reach the heights of the mountains in stature.

37.author: Mubarakpuri
37.text: And walk not on the earth with conceit and arrogance. Verily, you can neither rend nor penetrate the earth nor can you attain a stature like the mountains in height.

37.author: Pickthall
37.text: And walk not in the earth exultant. Lo! thou canst not rend the earth, nor canst thou stretch to the height of the hills.

37.author: Qarai
37.text: Do not walk exultantly on the earth. Indeed, you will neither pierce the earth, nor reach the mountains in height.

37.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
37.text: Do not walk proudly in the earth. Indeed, you will never tear open the earth, nor attain the height of mountains.

37.author: Saheeh International
37.text: And do not walk upon the earth exultantly. Indeed, you will never tear the earth [apart], and you will never reach the mountains in height.

37.author: Sarwar
37.text: Do not walk proudly on the earth; your feet cannot tear apart the earth nor are you as tall as the mountains.

37.author: Shakir
37.text: And do not go about in the land exultingly, for you cannot cut through the earth nor reach the mountains in height.

37.author: Wahiduddin Khan
37.text: Do not walk proudly on the earth. You cannot cleave the earth, nor can you rival the mountains in height.

37.author: Yusuf Ali
37.text: Nor walk on the earth with insolence: for thou canst not rend the earth asunder, nor reach the mountains in height.

37.author: Transliteration
37.text: Wala tamshi fee alardi marahan innaka lan takhriqa alarda walan tablugha aljibala toolan

38.author: Ahmed Ali
38.text: All these are evil and odious to your Lord.

38.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
38.text: Of all this related before, the evil among it is disliked by your Lord.

38.author: Arberry
38.text: All of that -- the wickedness of it is hateful in the sight of thy Lord has revealed to thee:

38.author: Daryabadi
38.text: Each of these--theyice thereof is unto thy Lord ever detestable.

38.author: Hilali & Khan
38.text: All the bad aspects of these (the above mentioned things) are hateful to your Lord.

38.author: Itani
38.text: The evil of all these is disliked by your Lord.

38.author: Maududi
38.text: The wickedness of each of that is hateful to your Lord.

38.author: Mubarakpuri
38.text: All the bad aspects of these (the above mentioned things) are hateful to your Lord.

38.author: Pickthall
38.text: The evil of all that is hateful in the sight of thy Lord.

38.author: Qarai
38.text: The evil of all these is detestable to your Lord.

38.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
38.text: The wickedness of all this is hateful with your Lord.

38.author: Saheeh International
38.text: All that - its evil is ever, in the sight of your Lord, detested.

38.author: Sarwar
38.text: All such things are sins and detestable in the sight of your Lord.

38.author: Shakir
38.text: All this-- the evil of it-- is hateful in the sight of your Lord.

38.author: Wahiduddin Khan
38.text: All that is evil in the sight of your Lord, and is detestable.

38.author: Yusuf Ali
38.text: Of all such things the evil is hateful in the sight of thy Lord.

38.author: Transliteration
38.text: Kullu thalika kana sayyiohu AAinda rabbika makroohan

39.author: Ahmed Ali
39.text: This is some of the wisdom your Lord has revealed to you. So do not take another god apart from God, or you will be cast into Hell, reproved, ostracised.

39.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
39.text: This is part of the divine revelations of wisdom that your Lord has sent down to you (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him); and O listener, do not set up another God with Allah, for you will then be thrown into hell – rebuked, rebuffed.

39.author: Arberry
39.text: That is of the wisdom thy Lord set not up with God another god, or thou wilt be cast into Gehenna, reproached and rejected.

39.author: Daryabadi
39.text: That is Part of that wisdom which thy Lord hath revealed unto thee, and set not up thou along with Allah anot her god, lest thou be cast into Hell, reproved, rejected.

39.author: Hilali & Khan
39.text: This is (part) of Al-Hikmah (wisdom, good manners and high character, etc.) which your Lord has inspired to you (O Muhammad SAW). And set not up with Allah any other ilah (god) lest you should be thrown into Hell, blameworthy and rejected, (from Allah's Mercy).

39.author: Itani
39.text: That is some of the wisdom your Lord has revealed to you. Do not set up with God another god, or else you will be thrown in Hell, rebuked and banished.

39.author: Maududi
39.text: That is part of the wisdom your Lord has revealed to you. So do not set up any deity beside Allah lest you are cast into Hell, rebuked and deprived of every good.

39.author: Mubarakpuri
39.text: This is (part) of Al-Hikmah (wisdom) which your Lord has revealed to you. And set not up with Allah any other god lest you should be thrown into Hell, blameworthy and rejected (from Allah's mercy).

39.author: Pickthall
39.text: This is (part) of that wisdom wherewith thy Lord hath inspired thee (O Muhammad). And set not up with Allah any other god, lest thou be cast into hell, reproved, abandoned.

39.author: Qarai
39.text: These are among [precepts] that your Lord has revealed to you of wisdom. Do not set up another god besides Allah, or you will be cast into hell, being blameworthy and banished [from His mercy].

39.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
39.text: That is of the wisdom your Lord has revealed to you. Do not set up with Allah another god, or you will be cast into Gehenna, condemned and rejected.

39.author: Saheeh International
39.text: That is from what your Lord has revealed to you, [O Muhammad], of wisdom. And, [O mankind], do not make [as equal] with Allah another deity, lest you be thrown into Hell, blamed and banished.

39.author: Sarwar
39.text: (Muhammad), these are words of wisdom which your Lord has revealed to you. Do not consider anything equal to God lest you be thrown into hell, despised, and driven away from God's mercy.

39.author: Shakir
39.text: This is of what your Lord has revealed to you of wisdom, and do not associate any other god with Allah lest you should be thrown into hell, blamed, cast away.

39.author: Wahiduddin Khan
39.text: This is part of the wisdom that your Lord has revealed to you. Do not set up any other deity with God, lest you be cast into Hell, condemned and rejected.

39.author: Yusuf Ali
39.text: These are among the (precepts of) wisdom, which thy Lord has revealed to thee. Take not, with Allah, another object of worship, lest thou shouldst be thrown into Hell, blameworthy and rejected.

39.author: Transliteration
39.text: Thalika mimma awha ilayka rabbuka mina alhikmati wala tajAAal maAAa Allahi ilahan akhara fatulqa fee jahannama malooman madhooran

40.author: Ahmed Ali
40.text: Has God chosen to give you sons, and taken for Himself daughters from among the angels? You utter grievous things indeed!

40.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
40.text: Has your Lord chosen sons for you, and created daughters for Himself from among the angels? Indeed you utter a profound word!

40.author: Arberry
40.text: What, has your Lord favoured you with sons and taken to Himself from the angels females? Surely it is a monstrous thing you are saying!

40.author: Daryabadi
40.text: Hath then your Lord distinguished you with sons and taken for Himself females from among the angels? Verily ye say a saying mighty?

40.author: Hilali & Khan
40.text: Has then your Lord (O pagans of Makkah) preferred for you sons, and taken for Himself from among the angels daughters. Verily! You utter an awful saying, indeed.

40.author: Itani
40.text: Has your Lord favored you with sons, while choosing for Himself daughters from among the angels? You are indeed saying a terrible thing.

40.author: Maududi
40.text: What, has your Lord favoured you with sons and has taken for Himself daughters from among the angels? You are indeed uttering a monstrous lie.

40.author: Mubarakpuri
40.text: Has then your Lord preferred for you sons, and taken for Himself from among the angels daughters Verily, you indeed utter an awful saying.

40.author: Pickthall
40.text: Hath your Lord then distinguished you (O men of Makka) by giving you sons, and hath chosen for Himself females from among the angels? Lo! verily ye speak an awful word!

40.author: Qarai
40.text: Did your Lord prefer you for sons, and [Himself] adopt females from among the angels? Indeed, you say a monstrous word!

40.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
40.text: What! Has your Lord favored you with sons and taken to Himself females from among the angels? Indeed, you utter a monstrous thing!

40.author: Saheeh International
40.text: Then, has your Lord chosen you for [having] sons and taken from among the angels daughters? Indeed, you say a grave saying.

40.author: Sarwar
40.text: (Pagans) has your Lord given you preference over Himself by granting you sons and taking the angels as His own daughters? What you say is a monstrous utterance.

40.author: Shakir
40.text: What! has then your Lord preferred to give you sons, and (for Himself) taken daughters from among the angels? Most surely you utter a grievous saying.

40.author: Wahiduddin Khan
40.text: What! Has your Lord then favoured you with sons and Himself adopted females from among the angels? What you say is monstrous.

40.author: Yusuf Ali
40.text: Has then your Lord (O Pagans!) preferred for you sons, and taken for Himself daughters among the angels? Truly ye utter a most dreadful saying!

40.author: Transliteration
40.text: Afaasfakum rabbukum bialbaneena waittakhatha mina almalaikati inathan innakum lataqooloona qawlan AAatheeman

41.author: Ahmed Ali
41.text: We have explained (the truth) in various ways in this Qur'an, that they may be warned; but it only increased their refractoriness.

41.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
41.text: And We have explained in this Qur’an in various ways, for them to understand; and it increases nothing except their hatred towards it.

41.author: Arberry
41.text: We have turned about in this Koran, that they may remember; and it increases them only in aversion.

41.author: Daryabadi
41.text: And assurelly We have propounded it variously in this Qur'an that they might be admonished, but it increaseth them only in aversion.

41.author: Hilali & Khan
41.text: And surely, We have explained [Our Promises, Warnings and (set forth many) examples] in this Quran that they (the disbelievers) may take heed, but it increases them in naught save aversion.

41.author: Itani
41.text: We have explained in this Quran in various ways, that they may remember, but it only adds to their rebellion.

41.author: Maududi
41.text: We have expounded (the Truth) in diverse ways in this Qur'an that they might take it to heart but all this only aggravates their aversion.

41.author: Mubarakpuri
41.text: And surely, We have explained in this Qur'an that they may take heed, but it increases them in naught save aversion.

41.author: Pickthall
41.text: We verily have displayed (Our warnings) in this Qur'an that they may take heed, but it increaseth them in naught save aversion.

41.author: Qarai
41.text: Certainly We have variously paraphrased [the principles of guidance] in this Quran so that they may take admonition, but it increases them only in aversion.

41.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
41.text: In this Koran We have clarified so that they may remember; but it has only increased their aversion.

41.author: Saheeh International
41.text: And We have certainly diversified [the contents] in this Qur'an that mankind may be reminded, but it does not increase the disbelievers except in aversion.

41.author: Sarwar
41.text: We have given you various facts (about the Truth in this Quran) so that they (unbelievers) would take heed, but this has only increased their aversion (to the truth).

41.author: Shakir
41.text: And certainly We have repeated (warnings) in this Quran that they may be mindful, but it does not add save to their aversion.

41.author: Wahiduddin Khan
41.text: We have explained [the truth] in this Quran in various ways, so that they may take heed, but it has only increased their aversion.

41.author: Yusuf Ali
41.text: We have explained (things) in various (ways) in this Qur'an, in order that they may receive admonition, but it only increases their flight (from the Truth)!

41.author: Transliteration
41.text: Walaqad sarrafna fee hatha alqurani liyaththakkaroo wama yazeeduhum illa nufooran

42.author: Ahmed Ali
42.text: Say: "Had there been other gods with Him, as they assert, they would surely have sought a way (of opposition) against the Lord of the Throne."

42.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
42.text: Proclaim (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), “If there were other Gods besides Him” – as they utter – “then they would have certainly found a way towards* the Owner of the Throne!”. (* To fight against Him.)

42.author: Arberry
42.text: Say: 'If there had been other gods with Him, as they say, in that case assuredly they would have sought a way unto the Lord of the Throne.' Glory be to Him!

42.author: Daryabadi
42.text: Say thou: were there along with Him other gods, as they say, then they would have brought unto the Owner of the Throne a way.

42.author: Hilali & Khan
42.text: Say (O Muhammad SAW to these polytheists, pagans, etc.): "If there had been other aliha (gods) along with Him as they assert, then they would certainly have sought out a way to the Lord of the Throne (seeking His Pleasures and to be near to Him).

42.author: Itani
42.text: Say, “If there were other gods with Him, as they say, they would have sought a way to the Lord of the Throne.”

42.author: Maududi
42.text: Say, (O Muhammad): "Had there been other gods with Him, as they claim, they would surely have attempted to find a way to the Lord of the Throne.

42.author: Mubarakpuri
42.text: Say: "If there had been other gods along with Him as they assert, then they would certainly have sought out a way to the Lord of the Throne.

42.author: Pickthall
42.text: Say (O Muhammad, to the disbelievers): If there were other gods along with Him, as they say, then had they sought a way against the Lord of the Throne.

42.author: Qarai
42.text: Say, ‘Were there [other] gods besides Him, as they say, they would surely have encroached on the Lord of the Throne.

42.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
42.text: Say: 'If there had been other gods with Him, as they say, they would surely have sought a way to the Lord of the Throne'

42.author: Saheeh International
42.text: Say, [O Muhammad], "If there had been with Him [other] gods, as they say, then they [each] would have sought to the Owner of the Throne a way."

42.author: Sarwar
42.text: (Muhammad), ask them, "Had there been many other gods besides Him, as they say, they should have found a way to the Lord of the Throne

42.author: Shakir
42.text: Say: If there were with Him gods as they say, then certainly they would have been able to seek a way to the Lord of power.

42.author: Wahiduddin Khan
42.text: Say, "If there were [other] deities along with Him, as they claim, then they would surely have tried to find a way to the Lord of the Throne.

42.author: Yusuf Ali
42.text: Say: If there had been (other) gods with Him, as they say,- behold, they would certainly have sought out a way to the Lord of the Throne!

42.author: Transliteration
42.text: Qul law kana maAAahu alihatun kama yaqooloona ithan laibtaghaw ila thee alAAarshi sabeelan

43.author: Ahmed Ali
43.text: Too glorious and high is He, too exalted for what they say!

43.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
43.text: Purity and Supremacy are to Him from what they utter, a Great Supremacy!

43.author: Arberry
43.text: High indeed be He exalted above that they say!

43.author: Daryabadi
43.text: Hallowed be He, and exalted be He above that which they say --a greatheight!

43.author: Hilali & Khan
43.text: Glorified and High be He! From 'Uluwan Kabira (the great falsehood) that they say! (i.e. forged statements that there are other gods along with Allah, but He is Allah, the One, the Self-Sufficient Master, whom all creatures need. He begets not, nor was He begotten, and there is none comparable or coequal unto Him).

43.author: Itani
43.text: Be He glorified. He is exalted, far above what they say.

43.author: Maududi
43.text: Holy is He and far above all that they say.

43.author: Mubarakpuri
43.text: Glorified and Exalted is He high above what they say!

43.author: Pickthall
43.text: Glorified is He, and High Exalted above what they say!

43.author: Qarai
43.text: Immaculate is He, and greatly exalted above what they say!’

43.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
43.text: Exaltations be to Him! High indeed be He exalted above what they say!

43.author: Saheeh International
43.text: Exalted is He and high above what they say by great sublimity.

43.author: Sarwar
43.text: (to challenge Him). God is too Glorious and Exalted to be considered as they believe Him to be. He is the most High and Great.

43.author: Shakir
43.text: Glory be to Him and exalted be He in high exaltation above what they say.

43.author: Wahiduddin Khan
43.text: Glory be to Him! Exalted above all that they say!

43.author: Yusuf Ali
43.text: Glory to Him! He is high above all that they say!- Exalted and Great (beyond measure)!

43.author: Transliteration
43.text: Subhanahu wataAAala AAamma yaqooloona AAuluwwan kabeeran

44.author: Ahmed Ali
44.text: The seven skies, the earth, and all that lies within them, sing hallelujas to Him. There is nothing that does not chant His praises, but you do not understand their hymns of praise. He is verily clement and forgiving.

44.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
44.text: The seven heavens and the earth and all those in them say His Purity; and there is not a thing that does not proclaim His purity with praise, but you do not understand their proclamation of purity; indeed He is Most Forbearing, Oft Forgiving.

44.author: Arberry
44.text: The seven heavens and the earth, and whosoever in them is, extol Him; nothing is, that does not proclaim His praise, but you do not understand their extolling. Surely He is All-clement, All-forgiving.

44.author: Daryabadi
44.text: There hallow Him the seven heavens and the earth and whosoever is therein. And naught there is but halloweth His praise, but ye understand not their hallowing; verily He is ever Forbearing, Forgiving.

44.author: Hilali & Khan
44.text: The seven heavens and the earth and all that is therein, glorify Him and there is not a thing but glorifies His Praise. But you understand not their glorification. Truly, He is Ever Forbearing, Oft-Forgiving.

44.author: Itani
44.text: Praising Him are the seven heavens, and the earth, and everyone in them. There is not a thing that does not glorify Him with praise, but you do not understand their praises. He is indeed Forbearing and Forgiving.

44.author: Maududi
44.text: The seven heavens, the earth, and all that is within them give glory to Him. There is nothing but gives glory to Him with His praise, though you do not understand their hymns of praise. He is Most Forbearing, Exceedingly Forgiving."

44.author: Mubarakpuri
44.text: The seven heavens and the earth and all that is therein, glorify Him and there is not a thing but glorifies His praise. But you understand not their glorification. Truly, He is Ever Forbearing, Oft-Forgiving.

44.author: Pickthall
44.text: The seven heavens and the earth and all that is therein praise Him, and there is not a thing but hymneth His praise; but ye understand not their praise. Lo! He is ever Clement, Forgiving.

44.author: Qarai
44.text: The seven heavens glorify Him, and the earth [too], and whoever is in them. There is not a thing but celebrates His praise, but you do not understand their glorification. Indeed, He is all-forbearing, all-forgiving.

44.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
44.text: The seven heavens, the earth, and whosoever in them, exalt Him. There is nothing that does not exalt with His praise, but you do not understand their exaltation. Surely, He is Clement, Forgiving.

44.author: Saheeh International
44.text: The seven heavens and the earth and whatever is in them exalt Him. And there is not a thing except that it exalts [Allah] by His praise, but you do not understand their [way of] exalting. Indeed, He is ever Forbearing and Forgiving.

44.author: Sarwar
44.text: The seven heavens, the earth, and whatever is between them all glorify Him. There is nothing that does not glorify Him and always praise him, but you do not understand their praise and glorification. He is All-forbearing and All-forgiving.

44.author: Shakir
44.text: The seven heavens declare His glory and the earth (too), and those who are in them; and there is not a single thing but glorifies Him with His praise, but you do not understand their glorification; surely He is Forbearing, Forgiving.

44.author: Wahiduddin Khan
44.text: The seven heavens and the earth and all who dwell therein glorify Him. There is not a single thing but glorifies Him with His praise; but you do not understand their glorification. Truly, He is forbearing and most forgiving."

44.author: Yusuf Ali
44.text: The seven heavens and the earth, and all beings therein, declare His glory: there is not a thing but celebrates His praise; And yet ye understand not how they declare His glory! Verily He is Oft-Forbear, Most Forgiving!

44.author: Transliteration
44.text: Tusabbihu lahu alssamawatu alssabAAu waalardu waman feehinna wain min shayin illa yusabbihu bihamdihi walakin la tafqahoona tasbeehahum innahu kana haleeman ghafooran

45.author: Ahmed Ali
45.text: When you recite the Qur'an, We place a hidden veil between you and those who do not believe in the Hereafter;

45.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
45.text: And when you read the Qur’an O dear Prophet (Mohammed – peace and blessings upon him), We created an invisible barrier between you and those who do not believe in the Hereafter.

45.author: Arberry
45.text: When thou recitest the Koran, We place between thee, and those who do not believe in the world to come, a curtain obstructing,

45.author: Daryabadi
45.text: And when thou recitest the Qur'an, We set up between thee and those who believe not in the Hereafter a curtain drawn down.

45.author: Hilali & Khan
45.text: And when you (Muhammad SAW) recite the Quran, We put between you and those who believe not in the Hereafter, an invisible veil (or screen their hearts, so they hear or understand it not).

45.author: Itani
45.text: When you read the Quran, We place between you and those who do not believe in the Hereafter an invisible barrier.

45.author: Maududi
45.text: When you recite the Qur'an, We place a hidden barrier between you and those who do not believe in the Hereafter;

45.author: Mubarakpuri
45.text: And when you recite the Qur'an, We put between you and those who believe not in the Hereafter, an invisible veil.

45.author: Pickthall
45.text: And when thou recitest the Qur'an we place between thee and those who believe not in the Hereafter a hidden barrier;

45.author: Qarai
45.text: When you recite the Quran, We draw a hidden curtain between you and those who do not believe in the Hereafter,

45.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
45.text: When you recite the Koran, We place between you and those who do not believe in the Everlasting Life an obstructing barrier.

45.author: Saheeh International
45.text: And when you recite the Qur'an, We put between you and those who do not believe in the Hereafter a concealed partition.

45.author: Sarwar
45.text: When you recite the Quran We place a curtain as a barrier between you and those who do not believe in the life to come.

45.author: Shakir
45.text: And when you recite the Quran, We place between you and those who do not believe in the hereafter a hidden barrier;

45.author: Wahiduddin Khan
45.text: When you recite the Quran, We place an invisible barrier between you and those who do not believe in the Hereafter.

45.author: Yusuf Ali
45.text: When thou dost recite the Qur'an, We put, between thee and those who believe not in the Hereafter, a veil invisible:

45.author: Transliteration
45.text: Waitha qarata alqurana jaAAalna baynaka wabayna allatheena la yuminoona bialakhirati hijaban mastooran

46.author: Ahmed Ali
46.text: And We put covers over their hearts and deafness in their ears that they should not understand it. So when you invoke your Lord alone in the Qur'an they turn their backs and walk away.

46.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
46.text: And We placed covers upon their hearts so they may not understand it, and deafness in their ears; and when you mention your Only Lord in the Qur’an, they flee turning their backs in hatred.

46.author: Arberry
46.text: and We lay veils upon their hearts lest they understand it, and in their ears heaviness. And when thou mentionest thy Lord only in the Koran, they turn in their traces in aversion.

46.author: Daryabadi
46.text: And We set up over their hearts veils lest they understand it, and in their ears heaviness and when thou mentionest in reciting the Qur'an thy Lord alone, they turn backward as averters.

46.author: Hilali & Khan
46.text: And We have put coverings over their hearts lest, they should understand it (the Quran), and in their ears deafness. And when you make mention of your Lord Alone [La ilaha ill-Allah (none has the right to be worshipped but Allah) Islamic Monotheism] in the Quran, they turn on their backs, fleeing in extreme dislikeness.

46.author: Itani
46.text: And We drape veils over their hearts, preventing them from understanding it, and heaviness in their ears. And when you mention your Lord alone in the Quran, they turn their backs in aversion.

46.author: Maududi
46.text: and We place a covering on their hearts so that they do not comprehend it, and We cause a heaviness in their ears; and when you mention your Lord, the Only True Lord, in the Qur'an, they turn their backs in aversion.

46.author: Mubarakpuri
46.text: And We have put Akinnah over their hearts, lest they should understand it, and in their ears deafness. And when you make mention of your Lord Alone in the Qur'an, they turn on their backs, fleeing in extreme dislike.

46.author: Pickthall
46.text: And We place upon their hearts veils lest they should understand it, and in their ears a deafness; and when thou makest mention of thy Lord alone in the Qur'an, they turn their backs in aversion.

46.author: Qarai
46.text: and We cast veils on their hearts, lest they should understand it, and a deafness into their ears. When you mention your Lord alone in the Quran, they turn their backs in aversion.

46.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
46.text: We lay veils upon their hearts and heaviness in their ears, lest they understand it. When you (Prophet Muhammad) mention your Lord alone in the Koran, they turn their backs in aversion.

46.author: Saheeh International
46.text: And We have placed over their hearts coverings, lest they understand it, and in their ears deafness. And when you mention your Lord alone in the Qur'an, they turn back in aversion.

46.author: Sarwar
46.text: We put a veil over their hearts so that they cannot understand it. We deafen their ears. When you mention your Lord in this Quran as One (Supreme Being), they run away.

46.author: Shakir
46.text: And We have placed coverings on their hearts and a heaviness in their ears lest they understand it, and when you mention your Lord alone in the Quran they turn their backs in aversion.

46.author: Wahiduddin Khan
46.text: We put veils over their hearts to prevent them from comprehending it, and We afflict their ears with deafness. When you mention your one and only Lord in your recitation of the Quran, they turn their backs in aversion.

46.author: Yusuf Ali
46.text: And We put coverings over their hearts (and minds) lest they should understand the Qur'an, and deafness into their ears: when thou dost commemorate thy Lord and Him alone in the Qur'an, they turn on their backs, fleeing (from the Truth).

46.author: Transliteration
46.text: WajaAAalna AAala quloobihim akinnatan an yafqahoohu wafee athanihim waqran waitha thakarta rabbaka fee alqurani wahdahu wallaw AAala adbarihim nufooran

47.author: Ahmed Ali
47.text: We know well with what (intent) they hear you, for when they confer privately the wicked say: "You follow but a man deluded."

47.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
47.text: We know well why they listen when they lend ears to you, and when they discuss among themselves, when the unjust say, “You have not followed except a man who is under a magic spell.”

47.author: Arberry
47.text: We know very well how they listen when they listen to thee, and when they conspire, when the evildoers say, 'You are only following a man bewitched!'

47.author: Daryabadi
47.text: We are Best Knower of that wherewith they listen what time they listen to thee and what time they counsel together in secret, when the wrong-doers say: ye but follow a man enchanted.

47.author: Hilali & Khan
47.text: We know best of what they listen to, when they listen to you. And when they take secret counsel, behold, the Zalimun (polytheists and wrong-doers, etc.) say: "You follow none but a bewitched man."

47.author: Itani
47.text: We know well what they listen to, when they listen to you, as they conspire, when the wrongdoers say, “You only follow a man bewitched.”

47.author: Maududi
47.text: We are well aware of what they wish to hear when they listen to you and what they say when they confer in whispers, when the wrong-doers say: "You are only following one who is bewitched."

47.author: Mubarakpuri
47.text: We know best of what they listen to, when they listen to you. And when they take secret counsel, then the wrongdoers say: "You follow none but a bewitched man."

47.author: Pickthall
47.text: We are Best Aware of what they wish to hear when they give ear to thee and when they take secret counsel, when the evil-doers say: Ye follow but a man bewitched.

47.author: Qarai
47.text: We know best what they listen for, when they listen to you, and when they hold their secret talks, when the wrongdoers say, ‘[If you follow him] You will be following just a bewitched man.’

47.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
47.text: When they listen to you, We know very well how they listen. When they conspire, when the evildoers declare: 'You are only following a man who is bewitched'

47.author: Saheeh International
47.text: We are most knowing of how they listen to it when they listen to you and [of] when they are in private conversation, when the wrongdoers say, "You follow not but a man affected by magic."

47.author: Sarwar
47.text: We know what they want to hear when they listen to you. They whisper to each other and say, "You are only following a bewitched person".

47.author: Shakir
47.text: We know best what they listen to when they listen to you, and when they take counsel secretly, when the unjust say: You follow only a man deprived of reason.

47.author: Wahiduddin Khan
47.text: We are fully aware of what they wish to hear when they listen to you; and what they say when they converse in private; and when the wrongdoers say, "You are only following a man who is bewitched!"

47.author: Yusuf Ali
47.text: We know best why it is they listen, when they listen to thee; and when they meet in private conference, behold, the wicked say, "Ye follow none other than a man bewitched!"

47.author: Transliteration
47.text: Nahnu aAAlamu bima yastamiAAoona bihi ith yastamiAAoona ilayka waith hum najwa ith yaqoolu alththalimoona in tattabiAAoona illa rajulan mashooran

48.author: Ahmed Ali
48.text: See, what comparisons they coin for you, and go astray, and thus cannot find the way.

48.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
48.text: See the kind of examples they invent for you! They therefore strayed, so cannot find a path.

48.author: Arberry
48.text: Behold, how they strike similitudes for thee, and go astray, and cannot find a way!

48.author: Daryabadi
48.text: Behold! how they propound similitudes for thee! They have strayed and cannot find a way.

48.author: Hilali & Khan
48.text: See what examples they have put forward for you. So they have gone astray, and never can they find a way.

48.author: Itani
48.text: Note what they compared you to. They are lost, and unable to find a way.

48.author: Maududi
48.text: Just see how strange are the things they invent about you. They have altogether strayed, and are unable to find the right way.

48.author: Mubarakpuri
48.text: See what examples they have put forward for you. So they have gone astray, and never can they find a way.

48.author: Pickthall
48.text: See what similitudes they coin for thee, and thus are all astray, and cannot find a road!

48.author: Qarai
48.text: Look, how they coin epithets for you; so they go astray, and cannot find a way.

48.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
48.text: See what they compared you to. They have surely gone astray and cannot find a Path.

48.author: Saheeh International
48.text: Look how they strike for you comparisons; but they have strayed, so they cannot [find] a way.

48.author: Sarwar
48.text: Consider what they have called you. They have certainly gone astray and cannot find the right path.

48.author: Shakir
48.text: See what they liken you to! So they have gone astray and cannot find the way.

48.author: Wahiduddin Khan
48.text: See to what they liken you! But they are lost and cannot find the right path.

48.author: Yusuf Ali
48.text: See what similes they strike for thee: but they have gone astray, and never can they find a way.

48.author: Transliteration
48.text: Onthur kayfa daraboo laka alamthala fadalloo fala yastateeAAoona sabeelan

49.author: Ahmed Ali
49.text: Yet they say: "When we are turned to bones and bits, shall we be raised as a new creation?"

49.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
49.text: And they say, “When we have become bones and decomposed, will we really be raised up anew?”

49.author: Arberry
49.text: They say, 'What, when we are bones and broken bits, shall we really be raised up again in a new creation?'

49.author: Daryabadi
49.text: And they say: when we shall have become bones and fragments, shall we in sooth be raised as a new creation?

49.author: Hilali & Khan
49.text: And they say: "When we are bones and fragments (destroyed), should we really be resurrected (to be) a new creation?"

49.author: Itani
49.text: And they say, “When we have become bones and fragments, shall we really be resurrected as a new creation?”

49.author: Maududi
49.text: They say: "When we are turned to bones and particles (of dust), shall we truly be raised up as a new creation?"

49.author: Mubarakpuri
49.text: And they say: "When we are bones and fragments (destroyed), should we really be resurrected (to be) a new creation"

49.author: Pickthall
49.text: And they say: When we are bones and fragments, shall we forsooth, be raised up as a new creation?

49.author: Qarai
49.text: They say, ‘What, when we have become bones and dust, shall we really be raised in a new creation?’

49.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
49.text: 'What' they say. 'When we are (turned to) bones and broken bits, shall we be raised again in a new creation'

49.author: Saheeh International
49.text: And they say, "When we are bones and crumbled particles, will we [truly] be resurrected as a new creation?"

49.author: Sarwar
49.text: The pagans say, "When we become mere bones and dust, shall we then be brought back to life again?"

49.author: Shakir
49.text: And they say: What! when we shall have become bones and decayed particles, shall we then certainly be raised up, being a new creation?

49.author: Wahiduddin Khan
49.text: "What!" they say, "When we are turned to bones and dust, shall we be restored to life?"

49.author: Yusuf Ali
49.text: They say: "What! when we are reduced to bones and dust, should we really be raised up (to be) a new creation?"

49.author: Transliteration
49.text: Waqaloo aitha kunna AAithaman warufatan ainna lamabAAoothoona khalqan jadeedan

50.author: Ahmed Ali
50.text: Tell them: "(Even if) you turn to stones or steel,

50.author: Arberry
50.text: Say: 'Let you be stones, or iron,

50.author: Daryabadi
50.text: Say thou: become ye stones or iron.

50.author: Hilali & Khan
50.text: Say (O Muhammad SAW) "Be you stones or iron,"

50.author: Itani
50.text: Say, “Even if you become rocks or iron.

50.author: Maududi
50.text: Tell them: "(You will be raised afresh even if) you turn to stone or iron,

50.author: Pickthall
50.text: Say: Be ye stones or iron

50.author: Qarai
50.text: Say, ‘[That is bound to happen] even if you should become stones, or iron,

50.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
50.text: Say: 'Let you be stones or iron,

50.author: Sarwar
50.text: (Muhammad), say "Yes, even if you become rocks, iron,

50.author: Wahiduddin Khan
50.text: Say, "[yes] even if you turned to stones or iron,

50.author: Yusuf Ali
50.text: Say: "(Nay!) be ye stones or iron,

51.author: Ahmed Ali
51.text: Or some other created thing which may seem to you most difficult (to create)!" They will then say: "Who will revert us back?" Say: "He who originated you in the first place." They will shake their heads at you and say: "When will that be?" Say: "In the near future, perhaps,

51.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
51.text: “Or some other creation which you deem great”; so they will now say, “Who will create us again?”; proclaim, “He Who created you for the first time”; so now they will mockingly shake their heads at you, and question, “When is this going to occur?”; say, “It could perhaps be soon.”

51.author: Arberry
51.text: or some creation yet more monstrous in your minds!' Then they will say, 'Who will bring us back?' Say: 'He who originated you the first time.' Then they will shake their heads at thee, and they will say, 'When will it be?' Say: 'It is possible that it may be nigh,

51.author: Daryabadi
51.text: Or anything created of the things more remote in your breasts. Then they will say: who will restore us! Say thou: He who created you the first time. Then they will wag their heads at thee and say; when will it be! Say thou: belike it is nigh-

51.author: Hilali & Khan
51.text: "Or some created thing that is yet greater (or harder) in your breasts (thoughts to be resurrected, even then you shall be resurrected)" Then, they will say: "Who shall bring us back (to life)?" Say: "He Who created you first!" Then, they will shake their heads at you and say: "When will that be?" Say: "Perhaps it is near!"

51.author: Itani
51.text: Or some substance, which, in your minds, is even harder.” Then they will say, “Who will restore us?” Say, “The One who originated you the first time.” Then they will nod their heads at you, and say, “When will it be?” Say, “Perhaps it will be soon.”

51.author: Maududi
51.text: or any other form of creation you deem hardest of all (to recreate from)." They will certainly ask: "Who will bring us back (to life)?" Say: "He Who created you in the first instance." They will shake their heads at you and inquire: "When will that be?" Say: "Perhaps that time might have drawn near;

51.author: Mubarakpuri
51.text: "Or some created thing that is yet greater (or harder) in your breasts." Then, they will say: "Who shall bring us back (to life)" Say: "He Who created you first!" Then, they will shake their heads at you and say: "When will that be" Say: "Perhaps it is near!"

51.author: Pickthall
51.text: Or some created thing that is yet greater in your thoughts! Then they will say: Who shall bring us back (to life). Say: He Who created you at the first. Then will they shake their heads at thee, and say: When will it be? Say: It will perhaps be soon;

51.author: Qarai
51.text: or a creature more fantastic to your minds!’ They will say, ‘Who will bring us back?’ Say, ‘He who originated you the first time.’ They will nod their heads at you, and say, ‘When will that be?’ Say, ‘Maybe it is near!

51.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
51.text: or any other creation yet more monstrous in your minds' They will ask: 'Who will restore us' Say: 'He who originated you at first' They will shake theirheads and ask: 'When will this be' Say: 'Maybe it is near,

51.author: Saheeh International
51.text: Or [any] creation of that which is great within your breasts." And they will say, "Who will restore us?" Say, "He who brought you forth the first time." Then they will nod their heads toward you and say, "When is that?" Say, "Perhaps it will be soon -

51.author: Sarwar
51.text: or anything that you think is harder to be brought to life." They will soon ask, "Who will bring us back to life?" Say,"The One who created you in the first place." They will shake their heads and say, "When will He bring us back to life?" Say, "Perhaps very soon.

51.author: Shakir
51.text: Or some other creature of those which are too hard (to receive life) in your minds! But they will say: Who will return us? Say: Who created you at first. Still they will shake their heads at you and say: When will it be? Say: Maybe it has drawn nigh.

51.author: Wahiduddin Khan
51.text: or any other substance which you think unlikely to be given life." Then they will ask, "Who is it that shall restore us to life?" Answer them, "He who created you the first time." They will then shake their heads at you and say, "When will that be?" Say, "It may well be very soon."

51.author: Yusuf Ali
51.text: "Or created matter which, in your minds, is hardest (to be raised up),- (Yet shall ye be raised up)!" then will they say: "Who will cause us to return?" Say: "He who created you first!" Then will they wag their heads towards thee, and say, "When will that be?" Say, "May be it will be quite soon!

51.author: Transliteration
51.text: Aw khalqan mimma yakburu fee sudoorikum fasayaqooloona man yuAAeeduna quli allathee fatarakum awwala marratin fasayunghidoona ilayka ruoosahum wayaqooloona mata huwa qul AAasa an yakoona qareeban

52.author: Ahmed Ali
52.text: "On the day when He will call you, and you will answer with His praises and imagine you did not tarry but a while."

52.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
52.text: “The day when He will call you and you will come praising Him, thinking you have stayed only a little.”

52.author: Arberry
52.text: on the day when He will call you, and you will answer praising Him, and you will think you have but tarried a little.'

52.author: Daryabadi
52.text: The Day whereon He will call you, and ye will answerl with His praise, and ye will imagine that ye had tarried but little.

52.author: Hilali & Khan
52.text: On the Day when He will call you, and you will answer (His Call) with (words of) His Praise and Obedience, and you will think that you have stayed (in this world) but a little while!

52.author: Itani
52.text: On the Day when He calls you, you will respond with His praise, and you will realize that you stayed only a little.

52.author: Maududi
52.text: on the Day when He will call you and you will rise praising Him in response to His call, and you will believe that you had lain in this state only for a while."

52.author: Mubarakpuri
52.text: On the Day when He will call you, and you will answer with (words of) His praise and obedience, and you will think that you have stayed (in this world) but a little while!

52.author: Pickthall
52.text: A day when He will call you and ye will answer with His praise, and ye will think that ye have tarried but a little while.

52.author: Qarai
52.text: The day He calls you, you will respond to Him, praising Him, and you will think you remained [in the world] only for a little while.’

52.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
52.text: on that Day, He will summon you, and you shall answer Him with praise and you shall think you have stayed but for a little'

52.author: Saheeh International
52.text: On the Day He will call you and you will respond with praise of Him and think that you had not remained [in the world] except for a little."

52.author: Sarwar
52.text: On the day when He will call you, you will answer Him with praise and think that you have tarried for only a little while."

52.author: Shakir
52.text: On the day when He will call you forth, then shall you obey Him, giving Him praise, and you will think that you tarried but a little (while).

52.author: Wahiduddin Khan
52.text: On that Day He will call you, and you will answer by praising Him, thinking that you have stayed for only a little while."

52.author: Yusuf Ali
52.text: "It will be on a Day when He will call you, and ye will answer (His call) with (words of) His praise, and ye will think that ye tarried but a little while!"

52.author: Transliteration
