76.text: And indeed it was close that they frighten you in the land for them to oust you from it – and if it were, they would not have stayed after you, but a little.

76.author: Arberry
76.text: Indeed they were near to startling thee from the land, to expel thee from it, and then they would have tarried after thee only a little --

76.author: Daryabadi
76.text: And verily they had well-nigh unsettled thee from the land that they might drive thee forth from thence. And in that case they would not have tarried after thee but a little while.

76.author: Hilali & Khan
76.text: And Verily, they were about to frighten you so much as to drive you out from the land. But in that case they would not have stayed (therein) after you, except for a little while.

76.author: Itani
76.text: They almost provoked you, to expel you from the land. In that case, they would not have lasted after you, except briefly.

76.author: Maududi
76.text: They were bent upon uprooting you from this land and driving you away from it. But were they to succeed, they would not be able to remain after you more than a little while.

76.author: Mubarakpuri
76.text: And verily, they were about to frighten you so much as to drive you out from the land. But in that case, they would not have stayed (therein) after you, except for a little while.

76.author: Pickthall
76.text: And they indeed wished to scare thee from the land that they might drive thee forth from thence, and then they would have stayed (there) but a little after thee.

76.author: Qarai
76.text: They were about to hound you out of the land, to expel you from it, but then they would not have stayed after you but a little.

76.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
76.text: They very nearly provoked you to drive you out of the land, but they would have only lingered for a little while after you.

76.author: Saheeh International
76.text: And indeed, they were about to drive you from the land to evict you therefrom. And then [when they do], they will not remain [there] after you, except for a little.

76.author: Sarwar
76.text: They try to annoy you so that they can expel you from the land. Had they been successful, no one would have been left behind except a few.

76.author: Shakir
76.text: And surely they purposed to unsettle you from the land that they might expel you from it, and in that case they will not tarry behind you but a little.

76.author: Wahiduddin Khan
76.text: Indeed they came near to unsettling you, so that they might expel you from the land, but in that case they themselves would not have stayed on for very long after you.

76.author: Yusuf Ali
76.text: Their purpose was to scare thee off the land, in order to expel thee; but in that case they would not have stayed (therein) after thee, except for a little while.

76.author: Transliteration
76.text: Wain kadoo layastafizzoonaka mina alardi liyukhrijooka minha waithan la yalbathoona khilafaka illa qaleelan

77.author: Ahmed Ali
77.text: This has been Our way with the apostles whom We sent before you. You will not find any variation in Our line of action.

77.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
77.text: The tradition of the Noble Messengers We sent before you – and you will not find Our rules changing.

77.author: Arberry
77.text: the wont of those We sent before thee of Our Messengers; thou wilt find no change to Our wont.

77.author: Daryabadi
77.text: This was Our dispensation with those whom We sent before thee of Our apostles, and thou wilt not find in this Our dispensation a change.

77.author: Hilali & Khan
77.text: (This was Our) Sunnah (rule or way) with the Messengers We sent before you (O Muhammad SAW), and you will not find any alteration in Our Sunnah (rule or way, etc.).

77.author: Itani
77.text: The tradition of the messengers We sent before you—you will find no change in Our rules.

77.author: Maududi
77.text: This has been Our Way with the Messengers whom We sent before you. You will find no change in Our Way.

77.author: Mubarakpuri
77.text: A Sunnah with which We sent Our Messengers before you and you will not find any alteration in Our Sunnah.

77.author: Pickthall
77.text: (Such was Our) method in the case of those whom We sent before thee (to mankind), and thou wilt not find for Our method aught of power to change.

77.author: Qarai
77.text: A precedent concerning those We have sent before you from among Our apostles, and you will not find any change in Our precedent.

77.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
77.text: Such was Our way with those whom We sent before you. You shall find no change in Our way.

77.author: Saheeh International
77.text: [That is Our] established way for those We had sent before you of Our messengers; and you will not find in Our way any alteration.

77.author: Sarwar
77.text: This was Our tradition with Our Messengers who lived before you, and you will find no change in Our tradition.

77.author: Shakir
77.text: (This is Our) course with regard to those of Our apostles whom We sent before you, and you shall not find a change in Our course.

77.author: Wahiduddin Khan
77.text: Such was Our way with the messengers We sent before you, and you will find no change in Our ways.

77.author: Yusuf Ali
77.text: (This was Our) way with the messengers We sent before thee: thou wilt find no change in Our ways.

77.author: Transliteration
77.text: Sunnata man qad arsalna qablaka min rusulina wala tajidu lisunnatina tahweelan

78.author: Ahmed Ali
78.text: Observe the service of prayer from the sun's declining from the meridian to the darkening of the night, and the recitation at dawn. Indeed the Recitation at dawn is palpably evident.

78.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
78.text: Keep the prayer established, from the declining of the sun until darkness of the night, and the Qur’an at dawn; indeed the angels witness the reading of the Qur’an at dawn.

78.author: Arberry
78.text: Perform the prayer at the sinking of the sun to the darkening of the night and the recital of dawn; surely the recital of dawn is witnessed.

78.author: Daryabadi
78.text: Establish thou the prayer from the declination of the sun to the darkening of the night, and the Recitation at the dawn; verily the Recitation at the dawn is ever borne witness to.

78.author: Hilali & Khan
78.text: Perform As-Salat (Iqamat-as-Salat) from mid-day till the darkness of the night (i.e. the Zuhr, 'Asr, Maghrib, and 'Isha' prayers), and recite the Quran in the early dawn (i.e. the morning prayer). Verily, the recitation of the Quran in the early dawn is ever witnessed (attended by the angels in charge of mankind of the day and the night).

78.author: Itani
78.text: Perform the prayer at the decline of the sun, until the darkness of the night; and the Quran at dawn. The Quran at dawn is witnessed.

78.author: Maududi
78.text: Establish Prayer from the declining of the sun to the darkness of the night; and hold fast to the recitation of the Qur'an at dawn, for the recitation of the Qur'an at dawn is witnessed.

78.author: Mubarakpuri
78.text: Perform the Salah from midday till the darkness of the night, and recite the Qur'an in the early dawn. Verily, the recitation of the Qur'an in the early dawn is ever witnessed.

78.author: Pickthall
78.text: Establish worship at the going down of the sun until the dark of night, and (the recital of) the Qur'an at dawn. Lo! (the recital of) the Qur'an at dawn is ever witnessed.

78.author: Qarai
78.text: Maintain the prayer [during the period] from the sun’s decline till the darkness of the night, and [observe particularly] the dawn recital. Indeed the dawn recital is attended [by angels].

78.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
78.text: Establish the prayer at the decline of the sun till the darkening of the night and the Koran recital at dawn. Surely, the Koran recital at dawn is witnessed.

78.author: Saheeh International
78.text: Establish prayer at the decline of the sun [from its meridian] until the darkness of the night and [also] the Qur'an of dawn. Indeed, the recitation of dawn is ever witnessed.

78.author: Sarwar
78.text: Say your prayer when the sun declines until the darkness of night and also at dawn. Dawn is certainly witnessed (by the angels of the night and day).

78.author: Shakir
78.text: Keep up prayer from the declining of the sun till the darkness of the night and the morning recitation; surely the morning recitation is witnessed.

78.author: Wahiduddin Khan
78.text: Say your prayers from the decline of the sun, until nightfall; and at dawn -- the recitation at dawn is indeed witnessed.

78.author: Yusuf Ali
78.text: Establish regular prayers - at the sun's decline till the darkness of the night, and the morning prayer and reading: for the prayer and reading in the morning carry their testimony.

78.author: Transliteration
78.text: Aqimi alssalata lidulooki alshshamsi ila ghasaqi allayli waqurana alfajri inna qurana alfajri kana mashhoodan

79.author: Ahmed Ali
79.text: Say a supererogatory prayer at the hour of the first watch: Your Lord may raise you to a most exalted station.

79.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
79.text: And forego sleep* in some part of the night – an increase for you**; it is likely your Lord will set you on a place where everyone will praise you***. (* For worship. ** Obligatory only upon the Holy Prophet. *** On the Day of Resurrection.)

79.author: Arberry
79.text: And as for the night, keep vigil a part of it, as a work of supererogation for thee; it may be that thy Lord will raise thee up to a laudable station.

79.author: Daryabadi
79.text: And of the night--keep the vigil therein, as an act of supererogation for thee; belike thy Lord will raise thee up in a station praised.

79.author: Hilali & Khan
79.text: And in some parts of the night (also) offer the Salat (prayer) with it (i.e. recite the Quran in the prayer), as an additional prayer (Tahajjud optional prayer Nawafil) for you (O Muhammad SAW). It may be that your Lord will raise you to Maqaman Mahmuda (a station of praise and glory, i.e. the highest degree in Paradise!).

79.author: Itani
79.text: And keep vigil with it during parts of the night, as an extra prayer. Perhaps your Lord will raise you to a laudable position.

79.author: Maududi
79.text: And rise from sleep during the night as well- this is an additional Prayer for you. Possibly your Lord will raise you to an honoured position.

79.author: Mubarakpuri
79.text: And in some parts of the night (also) offer the Salah with it as an additional prayer for you. It may be that your Lord will raise you to Maqam Mahmud.

79.author: Pickthall
79.text: And some part of the night awake for it, a largess for thee. It may be that thy Lord will raise thee to a praised estate.

79.author: Qarai
79.text: And keep vigil for a part of the night, as a supererogatory [devotion] for you. It may be that your Lord will raise you to a praiseworthy station.

79.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
79.text: As for the night there is a voluntary deed for you to keep vigil in part it. Perhaps your Lord will raise you to a praiseworthy station.

79.author: Saheeh International
79.text: And from [part of] the night, pray with it as additional [worship] for you; it is expected that your Lord will resurrect you to a praised station.

79.author: Sarwar
79.text: Say your special (tahajjud) prayer during some part of the night as an additional (obligatory) prayer for you alone so that perhaps your Lord will raise you to a highly praiseworthy position.

79.author: Shakir
79.text: And during a part of the night, pray Tahajjud beyond what is incumbent on you; maybe your Lord will raise you to a position of great glory.

79.author: Wahiduddin Khan
79.text: And during the night wake up and pray, as an additional prayer: it may well be that your Lord will raise you to a station of praise and glory."

79.author: Yusuf Ali
79.text: And pray in the small watches of the morning: (it would be) an additional prayer (or spiritual profit) for thee: soon will thy Lord raise thee to a Station of Praise and Glory!

79.author: Transliteration
79.text: Wamina allayli fatahajjad bihi nafilatan laka AAasa an yabAAathaka rabbuka maqaman mahmoodan

80.author: Ahmed Ali
80.text: And pray: "O my Lord, let my entry be with honour, and let my exit be with honour, and grant me power from You which would help (sustain) me."

80.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
80.text: And pray, “My Lord! Admit me with the truth and take me out with the truth*, and give me from Yourself a helpful dominance**.” (* Wherever I come or go ** Through spread of Islam.)

80.author: Arberry
80.text: And say: 'My Lord, lead me in with a just ingoing, and lead me out with a just outgoing; grant me authority from Thee, to help me.'

80.author: Daryabadi
80.text: And say thou: O my Lord! cause me to enter a rightful entrance and cause me to go forth with a rightful outgoing, and appoint for me from before Thee an authority helpful.

80.author: Hilali & Khan
80.text: And say (O Muhammad SAW): My Lord! Let my entry (to the city of Al-Madinah) be good, and likewise my exit (from the city of Makkah) be good. And grant me from You an authority to help me (or a firm sign or a proof).

80.author: Itani
80.text: And say, “My Lord, lead me in through an entry of truth, and lead me out through an exit of truth, and grant me from You a supporting power.”

80.author: Maududi
80.text: And pray: "My Lord! Cause me to enter wherever it be, with Truth, and cause me to exit, wherever it be, with Truth, and support me with authority from Yourself."

80.author: Mubarakpuri
80.text: And say: "My Lord! Let my entry be good, and (likewise) my exit be good. And grant me from You a helping authority,"

80.author: Pickthall
80.text: And say: My Lord! Cause me to come in with a firm incoming and to go out with a firm outgoing. And give me from Thy presence a sustaining Power.

80.author: Qarai
80.text: And say, ‘My Lord! ‘Admit me with a worthy entrance, and bring me out with a worthy departure, and render me a favourable authority from Yourself.’

80.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
80.text: Say: 'Lord, grant me an entrance of sincerity and an exit of sincerity, and give me from Yours a victorious power'

80.author: Saheeh International
80.text: And say, "My Lord, cause me to enter a sound entrance and to exit a sound exit and grant me from Yourself a supporting authority."

80.author: Sarwar
80.text: (Muhammad), say, "Lord, make me enter through a path that will lead to the Truth and come out of an exit that will take me to the Truth. Give me helpful authority.

80.author: Shakir
80.text: And say: My Lord! make me to enter a goodly entering, and cause me to go forth a goodly going forth, and grant me from near Thee power to assist (me).

80.author: Wahiduddin Khan
80.text: Say, "Lord, grant me an honourable entrance and an honourable exit, and sustain me with Your power."

80.author: Yusuf Ali
80.text: Say: "O my Lord! Let my entry be by the Gate of Truth and Honour, and likewise my exit by the Gate of Truth and Honour; and grant me from Thy Presence an authority to aid (me)."

80.author: Transliteration
80.text: Waqul rabbi adkhilnee mudkhala sidqin waakhrijnee mukhraja sidqin waijAAal lee min ladunka sultanan naseeran

81.author: Ahmed Ali
81.text: And say: "Truth has come and falsehood nullified." Verily falsehood is perishable.

81.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
81.text: And proclaim, “The Truth has arrived and falsehood has vanished; indeed falsehood had to vanish.” (* With the arrival of the Last Prophet – Mohammed peace and blessings be upon him)

81.author: Arberry
81.text: And say: 'The truth has come, and falsehood has vanished away; surely falsehood is ever certain to vanish.'

81.author: Daryabadi
81.text: And say thou: the truth is come, and falsehood hath vanished; verily falsehood is ever vanishing.

81.author: Hilali & Khan
81.text: And say: "Truth (i.e. Islamic Monotheism or this Quran or Jihad against polytheists) has come and Batil (falsehood, i.e. Satan or polytheism, etc.) has vanished. Surely! Batil is ever bound to vanish."

81.author: Itani
81.text: And say, “The truth has come, and falsehood has withered away; for falsehood is bound to wither away.”

81.author: Maududi
81.text: And proclaim: "The Truth has come, and falsehood has vanished. Surely falsehood is ever bound to vanish."

81.author: Mubarakpuri
81.text: And say: "Truth has come and falsehood has vanished. Surely, falsehood is ever bound to vanish."

81.author: Pickthall
81.text: And say: Truth hath come and falsehood hath vanished away. Lo! falsehood is ever bound to vanish.

81.author: Qarai
81.text: And say, ‘The truth has come, and falsehood has vanished. Indeed falsehood is bound to vanish.’

81.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
81.text: Say: 'Truth has come and falsehood has vanished. Indeed, falsehood will certainly vanish'

81.author: Saheeh International
81.text: And say, "Truth has come, and falsehood has departed. Indeed is falsehood, [by nature], ever bound to depart."

81.author: Sarwar
81.text: Say, 'Truth has come and falsehood has been banished; it is doomed to banishment.'"

81.author: Shakir
81.text: And say: The truth has come and the falsehood has vanished; surely falsehood is a vanishing (thing).

81.author: Wahiduddin Khan
81.text: Say, "Truth has come and falsehood has disappeared. Falsehood is always bound to wither away."

81.author: Yusuf Ali
81.text: And say: "Truth has (now) arrived, and Falsehood perished: for Falsehood is (by its nature) bound to perish."

81.author: Transliteration
81.text: Waqul jaa alhaqqu wazahaqa albatilu inna albatila kana zahooqan

82.author: Ahmed Ali
82.text: What We have sent down of the Qur'an is a healing and a grace for the faithful, and adds only loss for the sinners.

82.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
82.text: And We send down in the Qur’an that which is a cure for the Muslims, and a mercy – and it increases only ruin for the unjust.

82.author: Arberry
82.text: And We send down, of the Koran, that which is a healing and a mercy to the believers; and the unbelievers it increases not, except in loss.

82.author: Daryabadi
82.text: And We reveal by means of the Qur'an that which is a healing and a mercy unto the believers, and it only increaseth the wrong- doers in loss.

82.author: Hilali & Khan
82.text: And We send down from the Quran that which is a healing and a mercy to those who believe (in Islamic Monotheism and act on it), and it increases the Zalimun (polytheists and wrong-doers) nothing but loss.

82.author: Itani
82.text: We send down in the Quran healing and mercy for the believers, but it increases the wrongdoers only in loss.

82.author: Maududi
82.text: What We are sending down in the course of revealing the Qur'an is a healing and a grace for those who have faith; but it adds only to the ruin of the wrong-doers.

82.author: Mubarakpuri
82.text: And We send down of the Qur'an that which is a cure and a mercy to the believers, and it increases the wrongdoers in nothing but loss.

82.author: Pickthall
82.text: And We reveal of the Qur'an that which is a healing and a mercy for believers though it increase the evil-doers in naught save ruin.

82.author: Qarai
82.text: We send down in the Quran that which is a cure and mercy for the faithful; and it increases the wrongdoers only in loss.

82.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
82.text: We sent down of the Koran that which is a healing and a mercy to believers, but to the harmdoers it does not increase them, except in loss.

82.author: Saheeh International
82.text: And We send down of the Qur'an that which is healing and mercy for the believers, but it does not increase the wrongdoers except in loss.

82.author: Sarwar
82.text: We reveal the Quran which is a cure and mercy for the believers but does nothing for the unjust except to lead them to perdition.

82.author: Shakir
82.text: And We reveal of the Quran that which is a healing and a mercy to the believers, and it adds only to the perdition of the unjust.

82.author: Wahiduddin Khan
82.text: We send down in the Quran that which is healing and a mercy to those who believe; as for the evil-doers, it only increases their loss.

82.author: Yusuf Ali
82.text: We send down (stage by stage) in the Qur'an that which is a healing and a mercy to those who believe: to the unjust it causes nothing but loss after loss.

82.author: Transliteration
82.text: Wanunazzilu mina alqurani ma huwa shifaon warahmatun lilmumineena wala yazeedu alththalimeena illa khasaran

83.author: Ahmed Ali
83.text: When We are gracious to man he turns away and moves aside; yet when evil befalls him he begins to despair.

83.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
83.text: And when We bestow favours upon man, he turns away and goes far away towards himself; and when evil touches him he despairs.

83.author: Arberry
83.text: And when We bless man, he turns away, and withdraws aside; but when evil visits him, he is in despair.

83.author: Daryabadi
83.text: And when We show favour unto he turneth away and withdraweth on his side; and when evil toucheth him he is ever despairing.

83.author: Hilali & Khan
83.text: And when We bestow Our Grace on man (the disbeliever), he turns away and becomes arrogant, far away from the Right Path. And when evil touches him he is in great despair.

83.author: Itani
83.text: When We bless the human being, he turns away and distances himself. But when adversity touches him, he is in despair.

83.author: Maududi
83.text: Whenever We bestow favours upon man, he arrogantly turns away and draws aside; and whenever evil visits him, he is in utter despair.

83.author: Mubarakpuri
83.text: And when We bestow Our grace on man, he turns away and becomes arrogant. And when evil touches him, he is in great despair.

83.author: Pickthall
83.text: And when We make life pleasant unto man, he turneth away and is averse; and when ill toucheth him he is in despair.

83.author: Qarai
83.text: When We bless man, he is disregardful and turns aside; but when an ill befalls him, he is despondent.

83.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
83.text: Yet when We bestow favors upon mankind he turns his back and withdraws to one side. But when evil befalls him, he despairs.

83.author: Saheeh International
83.text: And when We bestow favor upon the disbeliever, he turns away and distances himself; and when evil touches him, he is ever despairing.

83.author: Sarwar
83.text: When We do favors to the human being, he disregards it and turns away from it. When evil afflicts him, he becomes despairing.

83.author: Shakir
83.text: And when We bestow favor on man, he turns aside and behaves proudly, and when evil afflicts him, he is despairing.

83.author: Wahiduddin Khan
83.text: When We bestow a favour upon a person, he turns his back and draws aside; and when evil afflicts him he gives himself up to despair.

83.author: Yusuf Ali
83.text: Yet when We bestow Our favours on man, he turns away and becomes remote on his side (instead of coming to Us), and when evil seizes him he gives himself up to despair!

83.author: Transliteration
83.text: Waitha anAAamna AAala alinsani aAArada wanaa bijanibihi waitha massahu alshsharru kana yaoosan

84.author: Ahmed Ali
84.text: Say: "Each one acts according to his disposition, but your Lord knows well who follow the right path."

84.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
84.text: Proclaim, “Each one works according to his own pattern; and your Lord well knows him who is more upon guidance.”

84.author: Arberry
84.text: Say: 'Every man works according to his own manner; but your Lord knows very well what man is best guided as to the way.'

84.author: Daryabadi
84.text: Say thou: everyone worketh after his disposition, and your Lord is the Best Knower of him who is best guided on the Way.

84.author: Hilali & Khan
84.text: Say (O Muhammad SAW to mankind): "Each one does according to Shakilatihi (i.e. his way or his religion or his intentions, etc.), and your Lord knows best of him whose path (religion, etc.) is right."

84.author: Itani
84.text: Say, “Each does according to his disposition. Your Lord knows best who is better guided in the way.”

84.author: Maududi
84.text: Say, (O Prophet): "Each one acts according to his own manner. Your Lord knows well who is best-guided to the Right Path."

84.author: Mubarakpuri
84.text: Say: "Each one does according to Shakilatihi, and your Lord knows best of him whose path is right."

84.author: Pickthall
84.text: Say: Each one doth according to his rule of conduct, and thy Lord is Best Aware of him whose way is right.

84.author: Qarai
84.text: Say, ‘Everyone acts according to his character. Your Lord knows best who is better guided with regard to the way.’

84.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
84.text: Say: 'Each human works in his own manner. But your Lord knows very well who is best guided on the way'

84.author: Saheeh International
84.text: Say, "Each works according to his manner, but your Lord is most knowing of who is best guided in way."

84.author: Sarwar
84.text: Say, "Everyone does as he wants. Your Lord knows best who has the right guidance."

84.author: Shakir
84.text: Say: Every one acts according to his manner; but your Lord best knows who is best guided in the path.

84.author: Wahiduddin Khan
84.text: Say to them, "Everyone acts in his own way, and your Lord knows best who is rightly guided."

84.author: Yusuf Ali
84.text: Say: "Everyone acts according to his own disposition: But your Lord knows best who it is that is best guided on the Way."

84.author: Transliteration
84.text: Qul kullun yaAAmalu AAala shakilatihi farabbukum aAAlamu biman huwa ahda sabeelan

85.author: Ahmed Ali
85.text: They ask you about revelation. Say, revelation is by the command of your Lord, and that you have been given but little knowledge.

85.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
85.text: They ask you concerning the soul; proclaim “The soul is an entity by the command of my Lord, and you have not received knowledge except a little.”

85.author: Arberry
85.text: They will question thee concerning the Spirit. Say: 'The Spirit is of the bidding of my Lord. You have been given of knowledge nothing except a little.'

85.author: Daryabadi
85.text: And they ask thee regarding the spirit, Say thou: the spirit is by the command of my Lord, and ye have not been vouchsafed of knowledge save a little.

85.author: Hilali & Khan
85.text: And they ask you (O Muhammad SAW) concerning the Ruh (the Spirit); Say: "The Ruh (the Spirit): it is one of the things, the knowledge of which is only with my Lord. And of knowledge, you (mankind) have been given only a little."

85.author: Itani
85.text: And they ask you about the Spirit. Say, “The Spirit belongs to the domain of my Lord; and you were given only little knowledge.”

85.author: Maududi
85.text: They ask you about "the spirit". Say: "The spirit descends by the command of my Lord, but you have been given only a little knowledge."

85.author: Mubarakpuri
85.text: And they ask you concerning the Ruh (the spirit). Say: "The Ruh (the spirit) is one of the things, the knowledge of which is only with my Lord. And of knowledge, you (mankind) have been given only a little."

85.author: Pickthall
85.text: They are asking thee concerning the Spirit. Say: The Spirit is by command of my Lord, and of knowledge ye have been vouchsafed but little.

85.author: Qarai
85.text: They question you concerning the Spirit. Say, ‘The Spirit is of the command of my Lord, and you have not been given of the knowledge except a few [of you].’

85.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
85.text: They question you about the spirit. Say: 'The spirit is from the command of my Lord. Except for a little knowledge you have been given nothing'

85.author: Saheeh International
85.text: And they ask you, [O Muhammad], about the soul. Say, "The soul is of the affair of my Lord. And mankind have not been given of knowledge except a little."

85.author: Sarwar
85.text: They ask you about the Spirit. Say, "The Spirit comes by the command of my Lord. You have been given very little knowledge.

85.author: Shakir
85.text: And they ask you about the soul. Say: The soul is one of the commands of my Lord, and you are not given aught of knowledge but a little.

85.author: Wahiduddin Khan
85.text: They question you about the Spirit. Say, "The Spirit is at my Lord's command, and you have been granted but little knowledge."

85.author: Yusuf Ali
85.text: They ask thee concerning the Spirit (of inspiration). Say: "The Spirit (cometh) by command of my Lord: of knowledge it is only a little that is communicated to you, (O men!)"

85.author: Transliteration
85.text: Wayasaloonaka AAani alrroohi quli alrroohu min amri rabbee wama ooteetum mina alAAilmi illa qaleelan

86.author: Ahmed Ali
86.text: If We pleased We could take away what We have revealed to you. Then you will not find any one to plead for it with Us,

86.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
86.text: And if We willed We could have taken away the revelations which We have sent to you – you would then not find anyone who could advocate for you before Us for this.

86.author: Arberry
86.text: If We willed, We could take away that We have revealed to thee, then thou wouldst find none thereover to guard thee against Us,

86.author: Daryabadi
86.text: And if We listed, We could surely take away that which We have revealed unto thee: then thou wilt not find in respect thereof against us as trustee.

86.author: Hilali & Khan
86.text: And if We willed We could surely take away that which We have revealed to you by inspiration (i.e. this Quran). Then you would find no protector for you against Us in that respect.

86.author: Itani
86.text: If We willed, We could take away what We revealed to you. Then you will find for yourself no protecting guardian against Us.

86.author: Maududi
86.text: Had We willed, We could take away what We have revealed to you, then you would find none to help you in recovering it from Us.

86.author: Mubarakpuri
86.text: And if We willed, We could surely take away that which We have revealed to you. Then you would find no protector for you against Us in that respect.

86.author: Pickthall
86.text: And if We willed We could withdraw that which We have revealed unto thee, then wouldst thou find no guardian for thee against Us in respect thereof.

86.author: Qarai
86.text: If We wish, We would take away what We have revealed to you. Then you would not find for yourself any defender against Us,

86.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
86.text: If We pleased We could take away that which We have revealed to you then you should find none to guard you against Us,

86.author: Saheeh International
86.text: And if We willed, We could surely do away with that which We revealed to you. Then you would not find for yourself concerning it an advocate against Us.

86.author: Sarwar
86.text: Had We wanted, We could have removed the Spirit through whom We sent you revelation. Then you would not have found anyone to intercede with Us for you

86.author: Shakir
86.text: And if We please, We should certainly take away that which We have revealed to you, then you would not find for it any protector against Us.

86.author: Wahiduddin Khan
86.text: If We pleased, We would certainly take away that which We have revealed to you, then you would find no guardian for you against Us,

86.author: Yusuf Ali
86.text: If it were Our Will, We could take away that which We have sent thee by inspiration: then wouldst thou find none to plead thy affair in that matter as against Us,-

86.author: Transliteration
86.text: Walain shina lanathhabanna biallathee awhayna ilayka thumma la tajidu laka bihi AAalayna wakeelan

87.author: Ahmed Ali
87.text: Unless your Lord have mercy. His blessings on you are great indeed.

87.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
87.text: Except the mercy of your Lord; indeed His munificence upon you (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him) is extremely great.

87.author: Arberry
87.text: excepting by some mercy of thy Lord; surly His favour to thee is great.

87.author: Daryabadi
87.text: Except as a mercy from thy Lord verily His grace unto thee is ever great.

87.author: Hilali & Khan
87.text: Except as a Mercy from your Lord. Verily! His Grace unto you (O Muhammad SAW) is ever great.

87.author: Itani
87.text: Except through a mercy from your Lord. His favors upon you have been great.

87.author: Maududi
87.text: (Whatever you have received) is nothing but grace from your Lord. Indeed His favour to you is great.

87.author: Mubarakpuri
87.text: Except as a mercy from your Lord. Verily, His grace unto you is ever great.

87.author: Pickthall
87.text: (It is naught) save mercy from thy Lord. Lo! His kindness unto thee was ever great.

87.author: Qarai
87.text: except a mercy from your Lord. Indeed His grace has been great upon you.

87.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
87.text: except for Mercy from your Lord; for His favor to you is great indeed.

87.author: Saheeh International
87.text: Except [We have left it with you] as a mercy from your Lord. Indeed, His favor upon you has ever been great.

87.author: Sarwar
87.text: except by the mercy of your Lord. He has certainly bestowed great favors on you.

87.author: Shakir
87.text: But on account of mercy from your Lord-- surely His grace to you is abundant.

87.author: Wahiduddin Khan
87.text: except through the special mercy of your Lord. His favours towards you has been great indeed.

87.author: Yusuf Ali
87.text: Except for Mercy from thy Lord: for his bounty is to thee (indeed) great.

87.author: Transliteration
87.text: Illa rahmatan min rabbika inna fadlahu kana AAalayka kabeeran

88.author: Ahmed Ali
88.text: Say: "Surely if men and jinns get together to produce the like of this (Qur'an), they will not be able to produce the like of it, however they might assist one another.

88.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
88.text: Proclaim, “If all mankind and jinns agree to bring an equivalent to the Qur’an, they will not be able to bring its equal – even if they were to help each other.”

88.author: Arberry
88.text: Say: 'If men and jinn banded together to produce the like of this Koran, they would never produce its like, not though they backed one another.'

88.author: Daryabadi
88.text: Say thou: if the mankind and the jinn leagued together that they might produce the like of this Qur'an, they could nor produce the like thereof, though one to the other were a backer.

88.author: Hilali & Khan
88.text: Say: "If the mankind and the jinns were together to produce the like of this Quran, they could not produce the like thereof, even if they helped one another."

88.author: Itani
88.text: Say, “If mankind and jinn came together to produce the like of this Quran, they could never produce the like of it, even if they backed up one another.”

88.author: Maududi
88.text: Say: "Surely, if mankind and jinn were to get together to produce the like of this Qur'an, they will never be able to produce the like of it, howsoever they might help one another."

88.author: Mubarakpuri
88.text: Say: "If mankind and the Jinn were together to produce the like of this Qur'an, they could not produce the like thereof, even if they helped one another."

88.author: Pickthall
88.text: Say: Verily, though mankind and the jinn should assemble to produce the like of this Qur'an, they could not produce the like thereof though they were helpers one of another.

88.author: Qarai
88.text: Say, ‘Should all humans and jinn rally to bring the like of this Quran, they will not bring its like, even if they assisted one another.’

88.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
88.text: Say: 'If mankind and jinn combined together to produce the like of this Koran, they would never be able to produce one like it, not even if they were to help one another'

88.author: Saheeh International
88.text: Say, "If mankind and the jinn gathered in order to produce the like of this Qur'an, they could not produce the like of it, even if they were to each other assistants."

88.author: Sarwar
88.text: Say, "If all human beings and jinn were to come together to bring the equivalent of this Quran, they could not do so, even if they all were to help each other.

88.author: Shakir
88.text: Say: If men and jinn should combine together to bring the like of this Quran, they could not bring the like of it, though some of them were aiders of others.

88.author: Wahiduddin Khan
88.text: Say, "If all men and jinn gathered together to produce the like of this Quran, they could not produce one like it, however much they helped one another."

88.author: Yusuf Ali
88.text: Say: "If the whole of mankind and Jinns were to gather together to produce the like of this Qur'an, they could not produce the like thereof, even if they backed up each other with help and support.

88.author: Transliteration
88.text: Qul laini ijtamaAAati alinsu waaljinnu AAala an yatoo bimithli hatha alqurani la yatoona bimithlihi walaw kana baAAduhum libaAAdin thaheeran

89.author: Ahmed Ali
89.text: We have given examples of every kind to men in this Qur'an in various ways, and even then most men disdain everything but unbelief,

89.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
89.text: And indeed We have illustrated all kinds of examples in the Qur’an for mankind – so most men did not accept, except to be ungrateful.

89.author: Arberry
89.text: We have indeed turned about for men in this Koran every manner of similitude; yet most men refuse all but unbelief.

89.author: Daryabadi
89.text: And assuredly We have variously propounded for mankind in this Qur'an every kind of similitude, yet most men have refused everything except infidelity.

89.author: Hilali & Khan
89.text: And indeed We have fully explained to mankind, in this Quran, every kind of similitude, but most mankind refuse (the truth and accept nothing) but disbelief.

89.author: Itani
89.text: We have displayed for mankind in this Quran every kind of similitude, but most people insist on denying the truth.

89.author: Maududi
89.text: We have explained things for people in this Qur'an in diverse ways to make them understand the Message, yet most people obstinately persist in unbelief.

89.author: Mubarakpuri
89.text: And indeed We have fully explained to mankind, in this Qur'an, every kind of similitude, but most of mankind refuse (the truth and accept nothing) but disbelief.

89.author: Pickthall
89.text: And verily We have displayed for mankind in this Qur'an all kind of similitudes, but most of mankind refuse aught save disbelief.

89.author: Qarai
89.text: We have certainly interspersed this Quran with every [kind of] parable for the people, but most people are only intent on ingratitude.

89.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
89.text: Indeed, We have set forth for mankind in this Koran every kind of parable, yet most people refuse all except disbelief.

89.author: Saheeh International
89.text: And We have certainly diversified for the people in this Qur'an from every [kind] of example, but most of the people refused [anything] except disbelief.

89.author: Sarwar
89.text: We have mentioned in this Quran all kinds of examples for the human being, but most human beings turn away in disbelief.

89.author: Shakir
89.text: And certainly We have explained for men in this Quran every kind of similitude, but most men do not consent to aught but denying.

89.author: Wahiduddin Khan
89.text: In this Quran, We have set out all kinds of examples for people, yet most of them persist in denying the truth.

89.author: Yusuf Ali
89.text: And We have explained to man, in this Qur'an, every kind of similitude: yet the greater part of men refuse (to receive it) except with ingratitude!

89.author: Transliteration
89.text: Walaqad sarrafna lilnnasi fee hatha alqurani min kulli mathalin faaba aktharu alnnasi illa kufooran

90.author: Ahmed Ali
90.text: And say: "We will not believe you until you make a spring of water gush forth from the earth for us;

90.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
90.text: And they said, “We will not accept faith in you, until you cause a spring to gush forth from the earth for us.”

90.author: Arberry
90.text: They say, 'We will not believe thee till thou makest a spring to gush forth from the earth for us,

90.author: Daryabadi
90.text: And they say: we shall by no means believe in thee until thou causest for us to gush forth from the earth a foun tain.

90.author: Hilali & Khan
90.text: And they say: "We shall not believe in you (O Muhammad SAW), until you cause a spring to gush forth from the earth for us;

90.author: Itani
90.text: And they said, “We will not believe in you unless you make a spring burst from the ground for us.

90.author: Maududi
90.text: They said: "We shall not accept your Message until you cause a spring to gush forth for us from the earth;

90.author: Mubarakpuri
90.text: And they say: "We shall not believe in you, until you cause a spring to gush forth from the earth for us;"

90.author: Pickthall
90.text: And they say: We will not put faith in thee till thou cause a spring to gush forth from the earth for us;

90.author: Qarai
90.text: They say, ‘We will not believe you until you make a spring gush forth for us from the ground.

90.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
90.text: They say: 'We will not believe in you until you make a spring gush from the earth for us,

90.author: Saheeh International
90.text: And they say, "We will not believe you until you break open for us from the ground a spring.

90.author: Sarwar
90.text: They have said, "We shall never believe you until you cause a spring to gush forth from the earth,

90.author: Shakir
90.text: And they say: We will by no means believe in you until you cause a fountain to gush forth from the earth for us.

90.author: Wahiduddin Khan
90.text: They declare, "We will never believe in you until you cause a spring to flow for us from the earth;

90.author: Yusuf Ali
90.text: They say: "We shall not believe in thee, until thou cause a spring to gush forth for us from the earth,

90.author: Transliteration
90.text: Waqaloo lan numina laka hatta tafjura lana mina alardi yanbooAAan

91.author: Ahmed Ali
91.text: Or, until you acquire an orchard of date-palm trees and grapes, and produce rivers flowing through it;

91.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
91.text: “Or you have a garden of date-palms and grapes, and you make gushing rivers to flow in it.”

91.author: Arberry
91.text: or till thou possessest a garden of plants and vines, and thou makest rivers to gush forth abundantly all amongst it,

91.author: Daryabadi
91.text: Or there be for thee a garden of date-palms and wine, and thou causest in the midst thereof rivers to gush forth.

91.author: Hilali & Khan
91.text: "Or you have a garden of date-palms and grapes, and cause rivers to gush forth in their midst abundantly;

91.author: Itani
91.text: Or you have a garden of palms and vines; then cause rivers to gush pouring through them.

91.author: Maududi
91.text: or that there be a garden of palms and vines for you and then you cause rivers to abundantly flow forth through them;

91.author: Mubarakpuri
91.text: "Or you have a garden of date palms and grapes, and cause rivers to gush forth in their midst abundantly;"

91.author: Pickthall
91.text: Or thou have a garden of date-palms and grapes, and cause rivers to gush forth therein abundantly;

91.author: Qarai
91.text: Or until you have a garden of date palms and vines and you make streams gush through it.

91.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
91.text: or, until you own a garden of palms and vines and cause rivers to gush forth with abundant water in them;

91.author: Saheeh International
91.text: Or [until] you have a garden of palm tress and grapes and make rivers gush forth within them in force [and abundance]

91.author: Sarwar
91.text: or you (show) us that you have your own garden of palm trees and vines with flowing streams therein,

91.author: Shakir
91.text: Or you should have a garden of palms and grapes in the midst of which you should cause rivers to flow forth, gushing out.

91.author: Wahiduddin Khan
91.text: or you have a garden of date palms and vines, and cause streams to flow plentifully in the midst of them;

91.author: Yusuf Ali
91.text: "Or (until) thou have a garden of date trees and vines, and cause rivers to gush forth in their midst, carrying abundant water;

91.author: Transliteration
91.text: Aw takoona laka jannatun min nakheelin waAAinabin fatufajjira alanhara khilalaha tafjeeran

92.author: Ahmed Ali
92.text: Or, let chunks of sky fall over us, as you assert (you will); or, bring God and the angels as a surety;

92.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
92.text: “Or you cause the sky to fall upon us in pieces like you said – or bring Allah and the angels as Witness.”

92.author: Arberry
92.text: or till thou makest heaven to fall, as thou assertest, on us in fragments, or thou bringest God and the angels as a surety,

92.author: Daryabadi
92.text: Or thou causest the heaven to fall upon us, as thou assertest, in pieces, or thou bringest God and the angels face to face.

92.author: Hilali & Khan
92.text: "Or you cause the heaven to fall upon us in pieces, as you have pretended, or you bring Allah and the angels before (us) face to face;

92.author: Itani
92.text: Or make the sky fall on us in pieces, as you claim, or bring God and the angels before us.

92.author: Maududi
92.text: or cause the sky to fall on us in pieces as you claimed, or bring Allah and the angels before us, face to face;

92.author: Mubarakpuri
92.text: "Or you cause the heaven to fall upon us in pieces, as you have pretended, or you bring Allah and the angels before (us) face to face;"

92.author: Pickthall
92.text: Or thou cause the heaven to fall upon us piecemeal, as thou hast pretended, or bring Allah and the angels as a warrant;

92.author: Qarai
92.text: Or until you cause the sky to fall in fragments upon us, just as you have averred. Or until you bring Allah and the angels [right] in front of us.

92.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
92.text: or, until you cause the sky to fall upon us in pieces, as you have claimed, or, as a surety bring Allah with the angels in front;

92.author: Saheeh International
92.text: Or you make the heaven fall upon us in fragments as you have claimed or you bring Allah and the angels before [us]

92.author: Sarwar
92.text: or cause the sky to fall into pieces on us - as you believe you can - or bring God and the angels face to face with us,

92.author: Shakir
92.text: Or you should cause the heaven to come down upon us in pieces as you think, or bring Allah and the angels face to face (with us).

92.author: Wahiduddin Khan
92.text: or you cause the heavens to fall down on us in pieces, as you have claimed; or you bring God and the angels before us face to face;

92.author: Yusuf Ali
92.text: "Or thou cause the sky to fall in pieces, as thou sayest (will happen), against us; or thou bring Allah and the angels before (us) face to face:

92.author: Transliteration
92.text: Aw tusqita alssamaa kama zaAAamta AAalayna kisafan aw tatiya biAllahi waalmalaikati qabeelan

93.author: Ahmed Ali
93.text: Or, you come to possess a house of gold; or ascend to the skies, though we shall not believe in your having ascended till you bring down a book for us which we could read." Say: "Glory to my Lord. I am only man and a messenger."

93.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
93.text: “Or you have a house of gold, or you ascend up into heaven; and even then we shall not believe in your ascent unless you send down a book upon us, which we may read”; say (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), “Purity is to my Lord – who am I except a human, sent by Allah?” (* These can be done, only when Allah commands.)

93.author: Arberry
93.text: or till thou possessest a house of gold ornament, or till thou goest up into heaven; and we will not believe thy going up till thou bringest down on us a book that we may read. Say: 'Glory be to my Lord. Am I aught but a mortal, a Messenger?'

93.author: Daryabadi
93.text: Or there be for thee a house of gold or thou mountest to the heaven, and we will by no means believe in thy mounting even until thou causest a book to be sent down to us, which we may read. Say thou: hallowed be my Lord! I am naught but a human being sent as an apostle.

93.author: Hilali & Khan
93.text: "Or you have a house of adornable materials (like silver and pure gold, etc.), or you ascend up into the sky, and even then we will put no faith in your ascension until you bring down for us a Book that we would read." Say (O Muhammad SAW): "Glorified (and Exalted) be my Lord (Allah) above all that evil they (polytheists) associate with Him! Am I anything but a man, sent as a Messenger?"

93.author: Itani
93.text: Or you possess a house of gold. Or you ascend into the sky. Even then, we will not believe in your ascension, unless you bring down for us a book that we can read.” Say, “Glory be to my Lord. Am I anything but a human messenger?”

93.author: Maududi
93.text: or that there come to be for you a house of gold, or that you ascend to the sky - though we shall not believe in your ascension (to the sky) - until you bring down a book for us that we can read." Say to them, (O Muhammad): "Holy is my Lord! Am I anything else than a human being, who bears a Message (from Allah)?"

93.author: Mubarakpuri
93.text: "Or you have a house of Zukhruf, or you ascend up into the sky, and even then we will put no faith in your ascension until you bring down for us a Book that we would read." Say: "Glorified be my Lord! Am I anything but a man, sent as a Messenger"

93.author: Pickthall
93.text: Or thou have a house of gold; or thou ascend up into heaven, and even then we will put no faith in thine ascension till thou bring down for us a book that we can read. Say (O Muhammad): My Lord be Glorified! Am I aught save a mortal messenger?

93.author: Qarai
93.text: Or until you have a house of gold, or you ascend into the sky. And we will not believe your ascension until you bring down for us a book that we may read.’ Say, ‘Immaculate is my Lord! Am I anything but a human apostle?!’

93.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
93.text: or, until you possess an ornate house of gold, or ascend into the heavens; and we will not believe in your ascension until you have brought down for us a book which we can read' Say: 'Exaltations to my Lord! Am I anything except a human Messenger'

93.author: Saheeh International
93.text: Or you have a house of gold or you ascend into the sky. And [even then], we will not believe in your ascension until you bring down to us a book we may read." Say, "Exalted is my Lord! Was I ever but a human messenger?"

93.author: Sarwar
93.text: or (show us) that you have a well adorned house of your own, or climb into the sky. We shall never believe that you have climbed into the sky until you bring us a book that we can read." Say, "All glory belongs to my Lord. Am I more than a mortal Messenger?"

93.author: Shakir
93.text: Or you should have a house of gold, or you should ascend into heaven, and we will not believe in your ascending until you bring down to us a book which we may read. Say: Glory be to my Lord; am I aught but a mortal apostle?

93.author: Wahiduddin Khan
93.text: or you have a house made of gold; or you ascend to heaven; and we will not believe in your ascension until you send down to us a Book that we can read." Tell them, "Holy is my Lord. I am but a human being sent as a messenger."

93.author: Yusuf Ali
93.text: "Or thou have a house adorned with gold, or thou mount a ladder right into the skies. No, we shall not even believe in thy mounting until thou send down to us a book that we could read." Say: "Glory to my Lord! Am I aught but a man,- a messenger?"

93.author: Transliteration
93.text: Aw yakoona laka baytun min zukhrufin aw tarqa fee alssamai walan numina liruqiyyika hatta tunazzila AAalayna kitaban naqraohu qul subhana rabbee hal kuntu illa basharan rasoolan

94.author: Ahmed Ali
94.text: Nothing prevented men from believing when guidance came to them, other than (what) they said: "Has God sent (only) a man as messenger?"

94.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
94.text: And what prevented people from believing when the guidance came to them, except their saying that, “What! Allah has sent a human as a Noble Messenger?”?

94.author: Arberry
94.text: And naught prevented men from believing when the guidance came to them, but that they said, 'Has God sent forth a mortal as Messenger?'

94.author: Daryabadi
94.text: And naught hath prevented men from believing when the guidance came Unto them except that they said: hath God sent a human being as apostle?

94.author: Hilali & Khan
94.text: And nothing prevented men from believing when the guidance came to them, except that they said: "Has Allah sent a man as (His) Messenger?"

94.author: Itani
94.text: Nothing prevented the people from believing, when guidance has come to them, except that they said, “Did God send a human messenger?”

94.author: Maududi
94.text: Whenever Guidance came to people, nothing prevented them from believing except that they said: "Has Allah sent a human being as a Messenger?"

94.author: Mubarakpuri
94.text: And nothing prevented men from believing when the guidance came to them, except that they said: "Has Allah sent a man as (His) Messenger"

94.author: Pickthall
94.text: And naught prevented mankind from believing when the guidance came unto them save that they said: Hath Allah sent a mortal as (His) messenger?

94.author: Qarai
94.text: Nothing has kept these people from believing when guidance came to them, but their saying, ‘Has Allah sent a human as an apostle?!’

94.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
94.text: Nothing prevented people from believing when guidance came to them but (the excuse): 'Has Allah sent a human as a Messenger'

94.author: Saheeh International
94.text: And what prevented the people from believing when guidance came to them except that they said, "Has Allah sent a human messenger?"

94.author: Sarwar
94.text: What keeps people from belief that guidance has come to them, but they question, "Why has God sent a mortal Messenger?"

94.author: Shakir
94.text: And nothing prevented people from believing when the guidance came to them except that they said: What! has Allah raised up a mortal to be an apostle?

94.author: Wahiduddin Khan
94.text: Nothing has prevented men from believing whenever guidance came to them, save their query, "Has God sent a human being as a messenger?"

94.author: Yusuf Ali
94.text: What kept men back from belief when Guidance came to them, was nothing but this: they said, "Has Allah sent a man (like us) to be (His) Messenger?"

94.author: Transliteration
94.text: Wama manaAAa alnnasa an yuminoo ith jaahumu alhuda illa an qaloo abaAAatha Allahu basharan rasoolan

95.author: Ahmed Ali
95.text: Say: "If angels had peopled the earth and walked about in peace and quiet, We would surely have sent to them an angel as messenger.

95.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
95.text: Proclaim, “If there were angels walking peacefully on the earth, We would send down only an angel from heaven, as a Noble Messenger towards them.”

95.author: Arberry
95.text: Say: 'Had there been in the earth angels walking. at peace, We would have sent down upon them out of heaven an angel as Messenger.'

95.author: Daryabadi
95.text: Say thou: were there in the earth angels walking about contentedly, We would surely have sent down unto them from the heaven an angel as an apostle.

95.author: Hilali & Khan
95.text: Say: "If there were on the earth, angels walking about in peace and security, We should certainly have sent down for them from the heaven an angel as a Messenger."

95.author: Itani
95.text: Say, “If there were angels on earth, walking around in peace, We would have sent down to them from heaven an angel messenger.”

95.author: Maududi
95.text: Say: "Had angels been walking about in peace on the earth, We would surely have sent to them an angel from the heavens as Messenger."

95.author: Mubarakpuri
95.text: Say: "If there were on the earth, angels walking about in peace and security, We should certainly have sent down for them from the heaven an angel as a Messenger."

95.author: Pickthall
95.text: Say: If there were in the earth angels walking secure, We had sent down for them from heaven an angel as messenger.

95.author: Qarai
95.text: Say, ‘Had there been angels in the earth, walking around and residing [in it like humans do], We would have sent down to them an angel from the heaven as apostle.’

95.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
95.text: Say: 'Had there been angels walking at peace in the earth, We would have sent down an angel from heaven to them as a Messenger'

95.author: Saheeh International
95.text: Say, "If there were upon the earth angels walking securely, We would have sent down to them from the heaven an angel [as a] messenger."

95.author: Sarwar
95.text: (Muhammad), say, "Had the earth been inhabited by angels who would walk serenely therein, only then would We have sent to them angelic Messengers".

95.author: Shakir
95.text: Say: Had there been in the earth angels walking about as settlers, We would certainly have sent down to them from the heaven an angel as an apostle.

95.author: Wahiduddin Khan
95.text: Say, "If there had been angels walking around on earth, We would have sent an angel down from Heaven as a messenger for them."

95.author: Yusuf Ali
95.text: Say, "If there were settled, on earth, angels walking about in peace and quiet, We should certainly have sent them down from the heavens an angel for a messenger."

95.author: Transliteration
95.text: Qul law kana fee alardi malaikatun yamshoona mutmainneena lanazzalna AAalayhim mina alssamai malakan rasoolan

96.author: Ahmed Ali
96.text: Tell them: "God is sufficient witness between me and you, for He knows His creatures well, and is well-informed."

96.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
96.text: Proclaim, “Allah is Sufficient as Witness between me and you all; indeed He is Well Aware of, the Beholder of His bondmen.”

96.author: Arberry
96.text: Say: 'God suffices as a witness between me and you; surely He is aware of and sees His servants.'

96.author: Daryabadi
96.text: Say thou: Allah sufficeth as a witness between me and you; verily He is in respect of His bondmen ever the Aware, the Beholder.

96.author: Hilali & Khan
96.text: Say: "Sufficient is Allah for a witness between me and you. Verily! He is the All-Knower, the All-Seer of His slaves."

96.author: Itani
96.text: Say, “God is enough witness between you and me. He is fully aware of His servants, and He sees them well.”

96.author: Maududi
96.text: Tell them, (O Prophet): "Allah suffices as a witness between me and you. Allah is well aware and fully observes everything pertaining to His servants."

96.author: Mubarakpuri
96.text: Say: "Sufficient is Allah for a witness between me and you. Verily, He is Ever the All-Knower, the All-Seer of His servants."

96.author: Pickthall
96.text: Say: Allah sufficeth for a witness between me and you. Lo! He is Knower, Seer of His slaves.

96.author: Qarai
96.text: Say, ‘Allah suffices as witness between me and you. Indeed He is well aware His servants and a keen observer.’

96.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
96.text: Say: 'Allah is sufficient as a witness between me and you. He knows and observes His worshipers'

96.author: Saheeh International
96.text: Say, "Sufficient is Allah as Witness between me and you. Indeed he is ever, concerning His servants, Acquainted and Seeing."

96.author: Sarwar
96.text: Say, "God is a sufficient Witness between me and you. He certainly sees and knows all about His servants.

96.author: Shakir
96.text: Say: Allah suffices as a witness between me and you; surely He is Aware of His servants, Seeing.

96.author: Wahiduddin Khan
96.text: Say, "God suffices as a witness between me and you [all]. He is informed about and observant of His servants."

96.author: Yusuf Ali
96.text: Say: "Enough is Allah for a witness between me and you: for He is well acquainted with His servants, and He sees (all things).

96.author: Transliteration
96.text: Qul kafa biAllahi shaheedan baynee wabaynakum innahu kana biAAibadihi khabeeran baseeran

97.author: Ahmed Ali
97.text: He is guided whom God guides. As for him He allows to go astray, you will not find a protector other than Him. We shall raise them on the Day of Resurrection in their own image, blind and dumb and deaf: Their habitation will be Hell. Every time (its fire) subsides We will intensify its flame.

97.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
97.text: And only he whom Allah guides, is upon guidance; and whomever He sends astray – you will therefore not find for them any supporters besides Him; and We shall raise them by their faces on the Day of Resurrection – blind, dumb and deaf; their destination is hell; whenever it is about to extinguish, We will inflame it more for them.

97.author: Arberry
97.text: Whomsoever God guides, he is rightly guided; and whom He leads astray -- thou wilt not find for them protectors, apart from Him. And We shall muster them on the Resurrection Day upon their faces, blind, dumb, deaf; their refuge shall be Gehenna, and whensoever it abates We shall increase for them the Blaze.

97.author: Daryabadi
97.text: And whomsoever Allah guideth, then he is the guided; and whomsoever He sendeth astray--for such thou wilt by no means find friends beside Him. And We shall gather them on the Day of Judgment on their faces, blind and deaf and dumb; their abode being Hell; so oft as it grosweth dull We shall increase for them the Flame.

97.author: Hilali & Khan
97.text: And he whom Allah guides, he is led aright; but he whom He sends astray for such you will find no Auliya' (helpers and protectors, etc.), besides Him, and We shall gather them together on the Day of Resurrection on their faces, blind, dumb and deaf, their abode will be Hell; whenever it abates, We shall increase for them the fierceness of the Fire.

97.author: Itani
97.text: Whomever God guides is the guided one. And whomever He leaves astray—for them you will find no protectors apart from Him. And We will gather them on the Day of Resurrection, on their faces, blind, dumb, and deaf. Their abode is Hell; whenever it abates, We intensify the blaze for them.

97.author: Maududi
97.text: Whomsoever Allah guides is rightly guided; and whomsoever Allah lets go astray, you shall find none - apart from Him - who could protect him. We shall muster them all on the Day of Resurrection, on their faces - blind and dumb and deaf. Hell shall be their refuge. Every time its Fire subsides, We will intensify for them its flame.

97.author: Mubarakpuri
97.text: And he whom Allah guides, he is led aright; and whomever He leaves astray can never find helpers other than Him, and We shall gather them together on the Day of Resurrection on their faces, blind, dumb and deaf; their abode will be Hell; whenever it abates, We shall increase for them the fierceness of the Fire.

97.author: Pickthall
97.text: And he whom Allah guideth, he is led aright; while, as for him whom He sendeth astray, for them thou wilt find no protecting friends beside Him, and We shall assemble them on the Day of Resurrection on their faces, blind, dumb and deaf; their habitation will be hell; whenever it abateth, We increase the flame for them.

97.author: Qarai
97.text: Whomever Allah guides is rightly guided, and whomever He leads astray—you will never for find them any guardians besides Him. On the Day of Resurrection, We shall muster them [scrambling] on their faces, blind, dumb, and deaf. Their refuge shall be hell. Whenever it subsides, We shall intensify the blaze for them.

97.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
97.text: Those whom Allah guides are rightly guided; but for those whom He leads astray you shall find no guardian, other than Him. On the Day of Resurrection We shall gather all of them upon their faces, blind, dumb, deaf. Gehenna shall be their refuge, whenever it dwindles, We will increase the Blaze for them.

97.author: Saheeh International
97.text: And whoever Allah guides - he is the [rightly] guided; and whoever He sends astray - you will never find for them protectors besides Him, and We will gather them on the Day of Resurrection [fallen] on their faces - blind, dumb and deaf. Their refuge is Hell; every time it subsides We increase them in blazing fire.

97.author: Sarwar
97.text: Whomever God has guided has the proper guidance. You will never find any guardian besides God for the one whom He has caused to go astray. On the Day of Judgment, We will gather them lying on their faces, blind, dumb and deaf. Hell will be their dwelling. As hell fire abates, We will increase its blazing force.

97.author: Shakir
97.text: And whomsoever Allah guides, he is the follower of the right way, and whomsoever He causes to err, you shall not find for him guardians besides Him; and We will gather them together on the day of resurrection on their faces, blind and dumb and deaf; their abode is hell; whenever it becomes allayed We will add to their burning.

97.author: Wahiduddin Khan
97.text: Those whom God guides are the truly guided, and for those whom He lets go astray, you will find no helper besides Him, on the Day of Judgement. We shall gather them together, lying upon their faces, blind, dumb and deaf. Their abode shall be Hell. Every time the fire dies down, We will make it blaze up again for them.

97.author: Yusuf Ali
97.text: It is he whom Allah guides, that is on true Guidance; but he whom He leaves astray - for such wilt thou find no protector besides Him. On the Day of Judgment We shall gather, them together, prone on their faces, blind, dumb, and deaf: their abode will be Hell: every time it shows abatement, We shall increase from them the fierceness of the Fire.

97.author: Transliteration
97.text: Waman yahdi Allahu fahuwa almuhtadi waman yudlil falan tajida lahum awliyaa min doonihi wanahshuruhum yawma alqiyamati AAala wujoohihim AAumyan wabukman wasumman mawahum jahannamu kullama khabat zidnahum saAAeeran

98.author: Ahmed Ali
98.text: This will be their retribution for having denied Our signs and said: "Once we are turned to bones and bits, can we be raised as a new creation?"

98.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
98.text: This is their reward because they disbelieved in Our signs and said, “When we are bones and decomposed, will we really be created again and raised up again?”

98.author: Arberry
98.text: That is their recompense because they disbelieved in Our signs and said, 'What, when we are bones and broken bits, shall we really be raised up again in a new creation?'

98.author: Daryabadi
98.text: This shall be their meed because they disbelieved in Our signs and said when we have become bones and fragments, shall we in sooth be raised up a new creation!

98.author: Hilali & Khan
98.text: That is their recompense, because they denied Our Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) and said: "When we are bones and fragments, shall we really be raised up as a new creation?"

98.author: Itani
98.text: This is their repayment for having blasphemed against Our revelations, and having said, “Shall we, when we have become bones and fragments, be resurrected as a new creation?”

98.author: Maududi
98.text: That will be their recompense because they disbelieved in Our Signs and said: "What, when we shall be reduced to bones and particles (of dust), shall we be raised again as a new creation?"

98.author: Mubarakpuri
98.text: That is their recompense, because they denied Our Ayat and said: "When we are bones and fragments, shall we really be raised up as a new creation"

98.author: Pickthall
98.text: That is their reward because they disbelieved Our revelations and said: When we are bones and fragments shall we, forsooth, be raised up as a new creation?

98.author: Qarai
98.text: That is their requital because they defied Our signs and said, ‘What, when we have become bones and dust, shall we really be raised in a new creation?’

98.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
98.text: That will be their recompense because they disbelieved Our verses and said: 'When we are turned to bones and broken bits, shall we really be raised up once more as a new creation'

98.author: Saheeh International
98.text: That is their recompense because they disbelieved in Our verses and said, "When we are bones and crumbled particles, will we [truly] be resurrected [in] a new creation?"

98.author: Sarwar
98.text: This will be the recompense for their disbelief of Our revelations and for their saying, "Shall we be brought to life again after becoming bones and dust?"

98.author: Shakir
98.text: This is their retribution because they disbelieved in Our communications and said What! when we shall have become bones and decayed particles, shall we then indeed be raised up into a new creation?

98.author: Wahiduddin Khan
98.text: That is their recompense, because they rejected Our signs and asked, "When we are reduced to bones and dust, shall we indeed be raised up as a new creation?"

98.author: Yusuf Ali
98.text: That is their recompense, because they rejected Our signs, and said, "When we are reduced to bones and broken dust, should we really be raised up (to be) a new Creation?"

98.author: Transliteration
98.text: Thalika jazaohum biannahum kafaroo biayatina waqaloo aitha kunna AAithaman warufatan ainna lamabAAoothoona khalqan jadeedan

99.author: Ahmed Ali
99.text: Do they not perceive that God, who created the heavens and the earth, has the power to create the like of them? There is no doubt that. He has fixed a term for them. Even then the wicked disdain everything but unbelief.

99.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
99.text: Do they not see that Allah Who has created the heavens and the earth is Able to create people similar to them, and has set a term for them in which there is no doubt? So the unjust do not accept without being ungrateful.

99.author: Arberry
99.text: Have they not seen that God, who created the heavens and earth, is powerful to create the like of them? He has appointed for them a term, no doubt of it; yet the unbelievers refuse all but unbelief.

99.author: Daryabadi
99.text: Behold they not that Allah who created the heavens and the earth is Able to create their likes? And He hath appointed for them a term whereof there is no doubt; yet the wrong-doers have refused everything excepting infidelity.

99.author: Hilali & Khan
99.text: See they not that Allah, Who created the heavens and the earth, is Able to create the like of them. And He has decreed for them an appointed term, whereof there is not doubt. But the Zalimun (polytheists and wrong-doers, etc.) refuse (the truth the Message of Islamic Monotheism, and accept nothing) but disbelief.

99.author: Itani
99.text: Do they not consider that God, Who created the heavens and the earth, is Able to create the likes of them? He has assigned for them a term, in which there is no doubt. But the wrongdoers persist in denying the truth.

99.author: Maududi
99.text: Have they not perceived that Allah, Who has created the heavens and the earth, has the power to create the like of them? He has fixed a term for them about which there is no doubt. And yet the wrong-doers obstinately persist in unbelief.

99.author: Mubarakpuri
99.text: See they not that Allah, Who created the heavens and the earth, is able to create the like of them. And He has decreed for them an appointed term, whereof there is no doubt. But the wrongdoers refuse but disbelief.

99.author: Pickthall
99.text: Have they not seen that Allah Who created the heavens and the earth is Able to create the like of them, and hath appointed for them an end whereof there is no doubt? But the wrong-doers refuse aught save disbelief.

99.author: Qarai
99.text: Do they not see that Allah, who created the heavens and the earth, is able to create the like of them? He has appointed for them a term, in which there is no doubt; yet the wrongdoers are only intent on ingratitude.

99.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
99.text: Have they not seen that Allah, who has created the heavens and the earth, has power to create their like? There is no doubt that He has appointed for them aterm, yet the unbelievers refuse all but disbelief.

99.author: Saheeh International
99.text: Do they not see that Allah, who created the heavens and earth, is [the one] Able to create the likes of them? And He has appointed for them a term, about which there is no doubt. But the wrongdoers refuse [anything] except disbelief.

99.author: Sarwar
99.text: Do they not realize that God, who has created the heavens and the earth, has the power to create their like? He has given them life for an appointed time of which there is no doubt. The unjust turn away in disbelief (from Our revelation).

99.author: Shakir
99.text: Do they not consider that Allah, Who created the heavens and the earth, is able to create their like, and He has appointed for them a doom about which there is no doubt? But the unjust do not consent to aught but denying.

99.author: Wahiduddin Khan
99.text: Do they not see that God, who has created heavens and earth, is able to create the like of them? He has appointed a definite term for them; there is no doubt about it, but the wrongdoers persist in denying the truth.

99.author: Yusuf Ali
99.text: See they not that Allah, Who created the heavens and the earth, has power to create the like of them (anew)? Only He has decreed a term appointed, of which there is no doubt. But the unjust refuse (to receive it) except with ingratitude.

99.author: Transliteration
99.text: Awalam yaraw anna Allaha allathee khalaqa alssamawati waalarda qadirun AAala an yakhluqa mithlahum wajaAAala lahum ajalan la rayba feehi faaba alththalimoona illa kufooran

100.author: Ahmed Ali
100.text: Say: "Even if you owned the stores of the mercy of my Lord, you would have held them back for fear of spending them, for man is niggardly."

100.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
100.text: Proclaim, “If you owned the treasures of the mercy of my Lord, you would hoard them too for fear that they may get spent; and man is a big miser.”

100.author: Arberry
100.text: Say: 'If you possessed the treasuries of my Lord's mercy, yet would you hold back for fear of expending; and man is ever niggardly.'

100.author: Daryabadi
100.text: Say thou: if it were ye who owned the treasures of the mercy of my Lord, ye would surely refrain for fear of expending; and man is ever miserly.

100.author: Hilali & Khan
100.text: Say (to the disbelievers): "If you possessed the treasure of the Mercy of my Lord (wealth, money, provision, etc.), then you would surely hold back (from spending) for fear of (being exhausted), and man is ever miserly!"

100.author: Itani
100.text: Say, “If you possessed the treasuries of my Lord’s mercy, you would have withheld them for fear of spending.” The human being has always been stingy.

100.author: Maududi
100.text: Tell them, (O Prophet): "Even if you owned the treasures of my Lord's Mercy you would have held them back for fear of spending them." Man is indeed ever niggardly.

100.author: Mubarakpuri
100.text: Say: "If you possessed the treasure of the mercy of my Lord, then you would surely hold back for fear of spending, and man is ever miserly!"

100.author: Pickthall
100.text: Say (unto them): If ye possessed the treasures of the mercy of my Lord, ye would surely hold them back for fear of spending, for man was ever grudging.

100.author: Qarai
100.text: Say, ‘Even if you possessed the treasuries of my Lord’s mercy, you would withhold them for the fear of being spent, and man is very niggardly.’

100.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
100.text: Say: 'If you possessed the treasuries of my Lord's Mercy, you would hold them back for fear of spending and mankind is ever grudging!

100.author: Saheeh International
100.text: Say [to them], "If you possessed the depositories of the mercy of my Lord, then you would withhold out of fear of spending." And ever has man been stingy.

100.author: Sarwar
100.text: Say, "Had you owned the treasures of the Mercy of my Lord, you would have locked them up for fear of spending them. The human being has always been stingy.

100.author: Shakir
100.text: Say: If you control the treasures of the mercy of my Lord, then you would withhold (them) from fear of spending, and man is niggardly.

100.author: Wahiduddin Khan
100.text: Say, "Even if you possessed the treasures of the mercy of my Lord, you would surely hold them back for fear of spending. Man is indeed niggardly!"

100.author: Yusuf Ali
100.text: Say: "If ye had control of the Treasures of the Mercy of my Lord, behold, ye would keep them back, for fear of spending them: for man is (every) niggardly!"

100.author: Transliteration
100.text: Qul law antum tamlikoona khazaina rahmati rabbee ithan laamsaktum khashyata alinfaqi wakana alinsanu qatooran

101.author: Ahmed Ali
101.text: We gave Moses nine clear signs; so ask the children of Israel. When (Moses) came to them the Pharaoh said: "I think, O Moses, you have been deluded."

101.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
101.text: And indeed We gave Moosa nine clear signs, therefore ask the Descendants of Israel when he came to them – in response Firaun said, “O Moosa – I think you are under a magic spell.”

101.author: Arberry
101.text: And We gave Moses nine signs, clear signs. Ask the Children of Israel when he came to them, and Pharaoh said to him, 'Moses, I think thou art bewitched.'

101.author: Daryabadi
101.text: And assuredly We vouchsafed unto Musa nine manifest signs - ask thou the Children of Israel--so when he came unto them, Fir'awn said unto him, verily imagine thee, O Musa! enchanted.

101.author: Hilali & Khan
101.text: And indeed We gave to Musa (Moses) nine clear signs. Ask then the Children of Israel, when he came to them, then Fir'aun (Pharaoh) said to him: "O Musa (Moses)! I think you are indeed bewitched."

101.author: Itani
101.text: We gave Moses nine clear signs—ask the Children of Israel. When he went to them, Pharaoh said to him, “I think that you, Moses, are bewitched.”

101.author: Maududi
101.text: We granted Moses nine clear signs. Ask the Children of Israel about that: when these signs came forth, Pharaoh said to him: "Moses, I think that you are bewitched."

101.author: Mubarakpuri
101.text: And indeed We gave Musa nine clear signs. Ask then the Children of Israel, when he came to them, then Fir`awn said to him: "O Musa! I think you are indeed bewitched."

101.author: Pickthall
101.text: And verily We gave unto Moses nine tokens, clear proofs (of Allah's Sovereignty). Do but ask the Children of Israel how he came unto them, then Pharaoh said unto him: Lo! I deem thee one bewitched, O Moses.

101.author: Qarai
101.text: Certainly We gave Moses nine manifest signs. So ask the Children of Israel. When he came to them, Pharaoh said to him, ‘O Moses, indeed I think you are bewitched.’

101.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
101.text: To Moses We gave nine clear signs. Ask the Children of Israel about how he came to them. When Pharaoh said to him: 'Moses, I think you are bewitched.'

101.author: Saheeh International
101.text: And We had certainly given Moses nine evident signs, so ask the Children of Israel [about] when he came to them and Pharaoh said to him, "Indeed I think, O Moses, that you are affected by magic."

101.author: Sarwar
101.text: To Moses We gave nine illustrious miracles. Ask the Israelites; Moses came to them. The Pharaoh said to him,"Moses, I believe that you are bewitched".

101.author: Shakir
101.text: And certainly We gave Musa nine clear signs; so ask the children of Israel. When he came to them, Firon said to him: Most surely I deem you, O Musa, to be a man deprived of reason.

101.author: Wahiduddin Khan
