101.text: We did indeed give Moses nine manifest signs; you can enquire from the Children of Israel. When he came to them, Pharaoh said to him, "Moses, I can see that you are bewitched."

101.author: Yusuf Ali
101.text: To Moses We did give Nine Clear Signs: As the Children of Israel: when he came to them, Pharaoh said to him: "O Moses! I consider thee, indeed, to have been worked upon by sorcery!

101.author: Transliteration
101.text: Walaqad atayna moosa tisAAa ayatin bayyinatin faisal banee israeela ith jaahum faqala lahu firAAawnu innee laathunnuka ya moosa mashooran

102.author: Ahmed Ali
102.text: He replied: "You know that none but the Lord of the heavens and the earth has sent these (signs) as cogent proof. I truly think, O Pharaoh, your days are done."

102.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
102.text: He said, “You certainly know that these have not been sent down except by the Lord of the heavens and the earth, the eye-openers* for the hearts; and I think that you, O Firaun, will surely be ruined.” (* The signs which enlighten the hearts.)

102.author: Arberry
102.text: He said, 'Indeed thou knowest that none sent these down, except the Lord of the heavens and earth, as clear proofs; and, Pharaoh, I think thou art accursed.'

102.author: Daryabadi
102.text: Musa said: assuredly thou knowest that none hath sent down these save the Lord of the heavens and the earth as an enlightenment; and verily imagine thee, Fir'awn, doomed.

102.author: Hilali & Khan
102.text: [Musa (Moses)] said: "Verily, you know that these signs have been sent down by none but the Lord of the heavens and the earth as clear (evidences i.e. proofs of Allah's Oneness and His Omnipotence, etc.). And I think you are, indeed, O Fir'aun (Pharaoh) doomed to destruction (away from all good)!"

102.author: Itani
102.text: He said, “You know that none sent these down except the Lord of the heavens and the earth—eye openers; and I think that you, Pharaoh, are doomed.”

102.author: Maududi
102.text: Moses replied: "You know well that no one but the Lord of the heavens and the earth has sent these as eye-opening proofs. I truly think, O Pharaoh, that you are indeed doomed."

102.author: Mubarakpuri
102.text: He said: "Verily, you know that these signs have been sent down by none but the Lord of the heavens and the earth. And I think you are indeed, O Fir`awn, doomed to destruction!"

102.author: Pickthall
102.text: He said: In truth thou knowest that none sent down these (portents) save the Lord of the heavens and the earth as proofs, and lo! (for my part) I deem thee lost, O Pharaoh.

102.author: Qarai
102.text: He said, ‘You certainly know that no one has sent these [signs] except the Lord of the heavens and the earth, as eye-openers, and I, O Pharaoh, indeed think you are doomed.’

102.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
102.text: 'You know' he replied, 'that none except the Lord of the heavens and the earth has sent down these as clear proofs. Pharaoh, I believe you are destroyed.'

102.author: Saheeh International
102.text: [Moses] said, "You have already known that none has sent down these [signs] except the Lord of the heavens and the earth as evidence, and indeed I think, O Pharaoh, that you are destroyed."

102.author: Sarwar
102.text: He replied, "Certainly you have come to know that these have been sent by the Lord of the heavens and the earth as lessons to people. Pharaoh, I believe that you are doomed to perdition.

102.author: Shakir
102.text: He said: Truly you know that none but the Lord of the heavens and the earth has sent down these as clear proof and most surely I believe you, O Firon, to be given over to perdition.

102.author: Wahiduddin Khan
102.text: He said, "You know full well that none has sent down these signs but the Lord of the heavens and the earth as eye-opening evidence. Indeed, Pharaoh, I can see that you are doomed."

102.author: Yusuf Ali
102.text: Moses said, "Thou knowest well that these things have been sent down by none but the Lord of the heavens and the earth as eye-opening evidence: and I consider thee indeed, O Pharaoh, to be one doomed to destruction!"

102.author: Transliteration
102.text: Qala laqad AAalimta ma anzala haolai illa rabbu alssamawati waalardi basaira wainnee laathunnuka ya firAAawnu mathbooran

103.author: Ahmed Ali
103.text: Then he sought to turn them out of the land, but We drowned him and all his followers.

103.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
103.text: He therefore wished to expel them from the earth, so We drowned him and his companions, all together.

103.author: Arberry
103.text: He desired to startle them from the land; and We drowned him and those with him, all together.

103.author: Daryabadi
103.text: Then he besought to unsettle them from the land; wherefore We drowned him and those with him, all together.

103.author: Hilali & Khan
103.text: So he resolved to turn them out of the land (of Egypt). But We drowned him and all who were with him.

103.author: Itani
103.text: He resolved to scare them off the land, but We drowned him, and those with him, altogether.

103.author: Maududi
103.text: At last Pharaoh decided to uproot Moses and the Children of Israel from the land, but We drowned him together with all who were with him;

103.author: Mubarakpuri
103.text: So he resolved to turn them out of the land. But We drowned him and all who were with him.

103.author: Pickthall
103.text: And he wished to scare them from the land, but We drowned him and those with him, all together.

103.author: Qarai
103.text: He desired to exterminate them from the land, so We drowned him and all those who were with him.

103.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
103.text: Pharaoh sought to provoke them so that they would leave the land, but We drowned him, together with all who were with him.

103.author: Saheeh International
103.text: So he intended to drive them from the land, but We drowned him and those with him all together.

103.author: Sarwar
103.text: The Pharaoh wanted to expel the Israelites from the land so We drowned him and all who were with him.

103.author: Shakir
103.text: So he desired to destroy them out of the earth, but We drowned him and those with him all together;

103.author: Wahiduddin Khan
103.text: So he resolved to scare them out of the land: but We drowned him along with all those who were with him.

103.author: Yusuf Ali
103.text: So he resolved to remove them from the face of the earth: but We did drown him and all who were with him.

103.author: Transliteration
103.text: Faarada an yastafizzahum mina alardi faaghraqnahu waman maAAahu jameeAAan

104.author: Ahmed Ali
104.text: After this We told the children of Israel: "Dwell in the land. When the promise of reckoning comes, We shall bring you together from a motley crowd.

104.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
104.text: And after him, We said to the Descendants of Israel, “Reside in this land – then when the promise of the Hereafter comes, We will bring you all huddled together.”

104.author: Arberry
104.text: And We said to the Children of Israel after him, 'Dwell in the land; and when the promise of the world to come comes to pass, We shall bring you a rabble.'

104.author: Daryabadi
104.text: And We said, after him, unto the Children of Isra'l: dwell on the earth, then when there cometh the promise of the Hereafter, We shall bring you as a crowd.

104.author: Hilali & Khan
104.text: And We said to the Children of Israel after him: "Dwell in the land, then, when the final and the last promise comes near [i.e. the Day of Resurrection or the descent of Christ ['Iesa (Jesus), son of Maryam (Mary) on the earth]. We shall bring you altogether as mixed crowd (gathered out of various nations). [Tafsir Al-Qurtubi, Vol. 10, Page 338]

104.author: Itani
104.text: After him, We said to the Children of Israel, “Inhabit the land, and when the promise of the Hereafter arrives, We will bring you all together.”

104.author: Maududi
104.text: and thereafter We said to the Children of Israel: "Now dwell in the land, but when the promised time of the Hereafter comes, We shall bring you all together."

104.author: Mubarakpuri
104.text: And We said to the Children of Israel after him: "Dwell in the land, then, when the final and the last promise comes near, We shall bring you altogether as mixed crowd."

104.author: Pickthall
104.text: And We said unto the Children of Israel after him: Dwell in the land; but when the promise of the Hereafter cometh to pass We shall bring you as a crowd gathered out of various nations.

104.author: Qarai
104.text: After him We said to the Children of Israel, ‘Take up residence in the land, and when the occasion of the other [promise] comes, We shall gather you in mixed company.’

104.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
104.text: And thereafter We said to the Children of Israel: 'Dwell in the land. When the promise of the Everlasting Life comes We shall bring you all together'

104.author: Saheeh International
104.text: And We said after Pharaoh to the Children of Israel, "Dwell in the land, and when there comes the promise of the Hereafter, We will bring you forth in [one] gathering."

104.author: Sarwar
104.text: We told the Israelites after this to settle in the land until Our second promise will come true. We would then gather them all together (on the Day of Judgment).

104.author: Shakir
104.text: And We said to the Israelites after him: Dwell in the land: and when the promise of the next life shall come to pass, we will bring you both together in judgment.

104.author: Wahiduddin Khan
104.text: Thereafter, We said to the Israelites, "Dwell in the land. When the promise of the Hereafter comes to be fulfilled, We shall assemble you all together."

104.author: Yusuf Ali
104.text: And We said thereafter to the Children of Israel, "Dwell securely in the land (of promise)": but when the second of the warnings came to pass, We gathered you together in a mingled crowd.

104.author: Transliteration
104.text: Waqulna min baAAdihi libanee israeela oskunoo alarda faitha jaa waAAdu alakhirati jina bikum lafeefan

105.author: Ahmed Ali
105.text: We have sent it down with truth, and with truth has (the Qur'an) come down. And We have sent you only to give good news and to warn.

105.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
105.text: And We sent down the Qur’an with the truth, and it has come down only for the truth; and We did not send you except as a Herald of glad tidings and warnings.

105.author: Arberry
105.text: With the truth We have sent it down, and with the truth it has come down; and We have sent thee not, except good tidings to bear, and warning;

105.author: Daryabadi
105.text: And with truth We have sent it down and with truth it hath come down; and We have not sent thee but as a bringer of glad tidings and a warner.

105.author: Hilali & Khan
105.text: And with truth We have sent it down (i.e. the Quran), and with truth it has descended. And We have sent you (O Muhammad SAW) as nothing but a bearer of glad tidings (of Paradise, for those who follow your Message of Islamic Monotheism), and a warner (of Hell-fire for those who refuse to follow your Message of Islamic Monotheism).

105.author: Itani
105.text: With the truth We sent it down, and with the truth it descended. We sent you only as a bearer of good news and a warner.

105.author: Maududi
105.text: We have sent down the Qur'an with the Truth, and it is with the Truth that it has descended. And We have not sent you but to proclaim good news and to warn.

105.author: Mubarakpuri
105.text: And with truth We have sent it down, and with truth it has descended. And We have sent you as nothing but a bearer of glad tidings, and a warner.

105.author: Pickthall
105.text: With truth have We sent it down, and with truth hath it descended. And We have sent thee as naught else save a bearer of good tidings and a warner.

105.author: Qarai
105.text: With the truth did We send it down, and with the truth did it descend, and We did not send you except as a bearer of good news and as a warner.

105.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
105.text: We have sent it down with the truth, and with the truth it has come down. We have not sent you except a bearer of glad tidings and a warner,

105.author: Saheeh International
105.text: And with the truth We have sent the Qur'an down, and with the truth it has descended. And We have not sent you, [O Muhammad], except as a bringer of good tidings and a warner.

105.author: Sarwar
105.text: We sent it (the Quran) in all Truth and in all Truth it came. (Muhammad), We have sent you for no other reason than to be a bearer of glad news and a warner.

105.author: Shakir
105.text: And with truth have We revealed it, and with truth did it come; and We have not sent you but as the giver of good news and as a warner.

105.author: Wahiduddin Khan
105.text: We have revealed the Quran with the truth, and with the truth it has come down. We have sent you forth only to give good news and to give warning --

105.author: Yusuf Ali
105.text: We sent down the (Qur'an) in Truth, and in Truth has it descended: and We sent thee but to give Glad Tidings and to warn (sinners).

105.author: Transliteration
105.text: Wabialhaqqi anzalnahu wabialhaqqi nazala wama arsalnaka illa mubashshiran wanatheeran

106.author: Ahmed Ali
106.text: We have divided the Qur'an into parts that you may recite it to men slowly, with deliberation. That is why We sent it down by degrees.

106.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
106.text: And We sent down the Qur’an in parts, that you may gradually recite it to the people, and We sent it down slowly in stages.

106.author: Arberry
106.text: and a Koran We have divided, for thee to recite it to mankind at intervals, and We have sent it down successively.

106.author: Daryabadi
106.text: And this is a Recitation which We have made distinct that thou mayest recite it unto mankind with delay, and We have revealed it at intervals.

106.author: Hilali & Khan
106.text: And (it is) a Quran which We have divided (into parts), in order that you might recite it to men at intervals. And We have revealed it by stages. (in 23 years).

106.author: Itani
106.text: A Quran which We unfolded gradually, that you may recite to the people over time. And We revealed it in stages.

106.author: Maududi
106.text: We have revealed the Qur'an in parts that you may recite it to people slowly and with deliberation; and (for that reason) We have revealed it gradually (to suit particular occasions).

106.author: Mubarakpuri
106.text: And a Qur'an which We have divided (into parts), in order that you might recite it to men at intervals. And We have revealed it by stages.

106.author: Pickthall
106.text: And (it is) a Qur'an that We have divided, that thou mayst recite it unto mankind at intervals, and We have revealed it by (successive) revelation.

106.author: Qarai
106.text: We have sent the Quran in [discrete] parts so that you may recite it for the people a little at a time, and We have sent it down piecemeal.

106.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
106.text: and We have divided the Koran for you to recite at intervals to mankind and We have sent it down successively.

106.author: Saheeh International
106.text: And [it is] a Qur'an which We have separated [by intervals] that you might recite it to the people over a prolonged period. And We have sent it down progressively.

106.author: Sarwar
106.text: We have divided the Quran into many segments so that you would read them to the people in gradual steps as We reveal them to you from time to time.

106.author: Shakir
106.text: And it is a Quran which We have revealed in portions so that you may read it to the people by slow degrees, and We have revealed it, revealing in portions.

106.author: Wahiduddin Khan
106.text: We have revealed the Quran bit by bit so that you may recite it to the people slowly and with deliberation. We have imparted it by gradual revelation.

106.author: Yusuf Ali
106.text: (It is) a Qur'an which We have divided (into parts from time to time), in order that thou mightest recite it to men at intervals: We have revealed it by stages.

106.author: Transliteration
106.text: Waquranan faraqnahu litaqraahu AAala alnnasi AAala mukthin wanazzalnahu tanzeelan

107.author: Ahmed Ali
107.text: Say: "Believe in it or do not believe: Those who were given knowledge before it bow in adoration when it is read out to them,

107.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
107.text: Proclaim, “Whether you accept faith in it or not”; indeed those who received knowledge before the Qur’an came, fall down prostrate on their faces when it is recited to them.

107.author: Arberry
107.text: Say: 'Believe in it, or believe not; those who were given the knowledge before it when it is recited to them, fall down upon their faces prostrating,

107.author: Daryabadi
107.text: Say thou: whether ye believe it or believe it not, verily those who were vouchsafed knowledge before it, when it is recited unto them, fall down on their chins, prostrating.

107.author: Hilali & Khan
107.text: Say (O Muhammad SAW to them): "Believe in it (the Quran) or do not believe (in it). Verily! Those who were given knowledge before it (the Jews and the Christians like 'Abdullah bin Salam and Salman Al-Farisi), when it is recited to them, fall down on their faces in humble prostration."

107.author: Itani
107.text: Say, “Believe in it, or do not believe.” Those who were given knowledge before it, when it is recited to them, they fall to their chins, prostrating.

107.author: Maududi
107.text: Tell them, (O Prophet): "Whether you believe in it, or do not believe," but when it is recited to those who were given the knowledge before its revelation, they fall down upon their faces in prostration

107.author: Mubarakpuri
107.text: Say: "Believe in it or do not believe (in it). Verily, those who were given knowledge before it, when it is recited to them, fall down on their chins (faces) in humble prostration."

107.author: Pickthall
107.text: Say: Believe therein or believe not, lo! those who were given knowledge before it, when it is read unto them, fall down prostrate on their faces, adoring,

107.author: Qarai
107.text: Say, ‘Whether you believe in it, or do not believe in it, indeed when it is recited to those who were given knowledge before it, they fall down in prostration on their faces,

107.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
107.text: Say: 'Believe in it, or do not believe. When it is recited to those to whom knowledge was given before they fall prostrate upon their faces

107.author: Saheeh International
107.text: Say, "Believe in it or do not believe. Indeed, those who were given knowledge before it - when it is recited to them, they fall upon their faces in prostration,

107.author: Sarwar
107.text: Say, "It does not matter whether you believe in it or not, for when it is read to those who had received the knowledge (heavenly Books) that were sent before, they bow down and prostrate themselves before the Lord.

107.author: Shakir
107.text: Say: Believe in it or believe not; surely those who are given the knowledge before it fall down on their faces, making obeisance when it is recited to them.

107.author: Wahiduddin Khan
107.text: Say to them, "You may believe in it or not. Those to whom knowledge had been revealed, fall on their faces in prostration when it is recited,

107.author: Yusuf Ali
107.text: Say: "Whether ye believe in it or not, it is true that those who were given knowledge beforehand, when it is recited to them, fall down on their faces in humble prostration,

107.author: Transliteration
107.text: Qul aminoo bihi aw la tuminoo inna allatheena ootoo alAAilma min qablihi itha yutla AAalayhim yakhirroona lilathqani sujjadan

108.author: Ahmed Ali
108.text: And say: "Glory be to our Lord. The promise of our Lord has indeed been fulfilled."

108.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
108.text: And they say, “Purity is to our Lord – indeed the promise of our Lord had to come true.”

108.author: Arberry
108.text: and say, "Glory be to our Lord! Our Lord's promise is performed."

108.author: Daryabadi
108.text: And they say: hallowed be our Lord! the promise of Our Lord was ever to have been fulfilled.

108.author: Hilali & Khan
108.text: And they say: "Glory be to our Lord! Truly, the Promise of our Lord must be fulfilled."

108.author: Itani
108.text: And they say, “Glory to our Lord. The promise of our Lord is fulfilled.”

108.author: Maududi
108.text: and say: "Glory be to our Lord. Surely the promise of our Lord was bound to be fulfilled."

108.author: Mubarakpuri
108.text: And they say: "Glory be to our Lord! Truly, the promise of our Lord must be fulfilled."

108.author: Pickthall
108.text: Saying: Glory to our Lord! Verily the promise of our Lord must be fulfilled.

108.author: Qarai
108.text: and say, ‘‘Immaculate is our Lord! Indeed Our Lord’s promise is bound to be fulfilled.’’

108.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
108.text: and say, "Exaltations be to our Lord! The promise of our Lord is done.'

108.author: Saheeh International
108.text: And they say, "Exalted is our Lord! Indeed, the promise of our Lord has been fulfilled."

108.author: Sarwar
108.text: They say, "Our Lord is too Glorious to disregard His promise".

108.author: Shakir
108.text: And they say: Glory be to our Lord! most surely the promise of our Lord was to be fulfilled.

108.author: Wahiduddin Khan
108.text: and say, "Glory to our Lord! Our Lord's promise is bound to be fulfilled."

108.author: Yusuf Ali
108.text: "And they say: 'Glory to our Lord! Truly has the promise of our Lord been fulfilled!'"

108.author: Transliteration
108.text: Wayaqooloona subhana rabbina in kana waAAdu rabbina lamafAAoolan

109.author: Ahmed Ali
109.text: And weeping they kneel down, and this increases their humility.

109.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
109.text: And they fall down on their faces weeping, and this Qur’an increases their humility. (Command of prostration # 4).

109.author: Arberry
109.text: And they fall down upon their faces weeping; and it increases them in humility.'

109.author: Daryabadi
109.text: And they fall down on their chins weeping, and it increaseth them in humility.

109.author: Hilali & Khan
109.text: And they fall down on their faces weeping and it adds to their humility.

109.author: Itani
109.text: And they fall to their chins, weeping, and it adds to their humility.

109.author: Maududi
109.text: And they fall down upon their faces, weeping, and their humility increases when (the Qur'an) is recited to them.

109.author: Mubarakpuri
109.text: And they fall down on their chins (faces) weeping and it increases their humility.

109.author: Pickthall
109.text: They fall down on their faces, weeping, and it increaseth humility in them.

109.author: Qarai
109.text: Weeping, they fall down on their faces, and it increases them in humility.’

109.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
109.text: They fall down upon their chin, weeping and it increases them in humility.

109.author: Saheeh International
109.text: And they fall upon their faces weeping, and the Qur'an increases them in humble submission.

109.author: Sarwar
109.text: They bow down in prostration and weep and it makes them more humble (before the Lord).

109.author: Shakir
109.text: And they fall down on their faces weeping, and it adds to their humility.

109.author: Wahiduddin Khan
109.text: They fall down upon their faces weeping, and [the Quran] increases their humility."

109.author: Yusuf Ali
109.text: They fall down on their faces in tears, and it increases their (earnest) humility.

110.author: Ahmed Ali
110.text: Say: "Call Him Allah or call Him Ar-Rahman; whatever the name you call Him by, all His names are beautiful." Do not say your prayers too loudly or in too low a voice, but follow a middle coarse.

110.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
110.text: Proclaim, “Pray calling (Him) Allah or calling (Him) the Most Gracious; whichever name you call with – they are all His magnificent names; and do not offer your prayers very loudly or very softly, and seek a way between them.”

110.author: Arberry
110.text: Say: 'Call upon God, or call upon the Merciful; whichsoever you call upon, to Him belong the Names Most Beautiful.' And be thou not loud in thy prayer, nor hushed therein, but seek thou for a way between that.

110.author: Daryabadi
110.text: Say thou: call upon Allah or call upon Rahman, by whichsoever ye call, His are the excellent names. And shout not thy prayer, nor speak it low, but Seek between these a way.

110.author: Hilali & Khan
110.text: Say (O Muhammad SAW): "Invoke Allah or invoke the Most Beneficent (Allah), by whatever name you invoke Him (it is the same), for to Him belong the Best Names. And offer your Salat (prayer) neither aloud nor in a low voice, but follow a way between.

110.author: Itani
110.text: Say, “Call Him God, or call Him the Most Merciful. Whichever name you use, to Him belong the Best Names.” And be neither loud in your prayer, nor silent in it, but follow a course in between.

110.author: Maududi
110.text: Say to them (O Prophet!): "Call upon Him as Allah or call upon Him as al-Rahman; call Him by whichever name you will, all His names are beautiful. Neither offer your Prayer in too loud a voice, nor in a voice too low; but follow a middle course."

110.author: Mubarakpuri
110.text: Say: "Invoke Allah or invoke Ar-Rahman (the Most Gracious), by whatever name you invoke Him (it is the same), for to Him belong the Best Names. And offer your Salah (prayer) neither aloud nor in a low voice, but follow a way between.

110.author: Pickthall
110.text: Say (unto mankind): Cry unto Allah, or cry unto the Beneficent, unto whichsoever ye cry (it is the same). His are the most beautiful names. And thou (Muhammad), be not loud-voiced in thy worship nor yet silent therein, but follow a way between.

110.author: Qarai
110.text: Say, ‘Invoke ‘‘Allah’’ or invoke ‘‘the All-beneficent.’’ Whichever [of His Names] you may invoke, to Him belong the Best Names.’ Be neither loud in your prayer, nor murmur it, but follow a middle course between these,

110.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
110.text: Say: 'Call upon Allah, or call upon the Merciful; whichever (Name) you call upon, to Him belong the Most Beautiful Names' Pray neither loudly nor to quietly, rather, seek a middle course between them.

110.author: Saheeh International
110.text: Say, "Call upon Allah or call upon the Most Merciful. Whichever [name] you call - to Him belong the best names." And do not recite [too] loudly in your prayer or [too] quietly but seek between that an [intermediate] way.

110.author: Sarwar
110.text: (Muhammad), tell them, "It is all the same whether you call Him God or the Beneficent. All the good names belong to Him." (Muhammad), do not be too loud or slow in your prayer. Choose a moderate way of praying.

110.author: Shakir
110.text: Say: Call upon Allah or call upon, the Beneficent Allah; whichever you call upon, He has the best names; and do not utter your prayer with a very raised voice nor be silent with regard to it, and seek a way between these.

110.author: Wahiduddin Khan
110.text: Say, "Whether you call on God or on the Merciful One: His are the finest names." Pray neither in too loud a voice nor in silence, but between these two extremes. Seek a middle way

110.author: Yusuf Ali
110.text: Say: "Call upon Allah, or call upon Rahman: by whatever name ye call upon Him, (it is well): for to Him belong the Most Beautiful Names. Neither speak thy Prayer aloud, nor speak it in a low tone, but seek a middle course between."

110.author: Transliteration
110.text: Quli odAAoo Allaha awi odAAoo alrrahmana ayyan ma tadAAoo falahu alasmao alhusna wala tajhar bisalatika wala tukhafit biha waibtaghi bayna thalika sabeelan

111.author: Ahmed Ali
111.text: And say: "All praise be to God who has neither begotten a son nor has a partner in His kingdom; nor has He need of any one to protect Him from ignominy. So extol Him by extolling His majesty."

111.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
111.text: And say, “All praise is to Allah, Who has not chosen a son for Himself, and none is His partner in kingship, and none is His supporter due to weakness, and say ‘Allah is Great’ to proclaim His greatness.”

111.author: Arberry
111.text: And say: 'Praise belongs to God, who has not taken to Him a son, and who has not any associate in the Kingdom, nor any protector out of humbleness.' And magnify Him with repeated magnificats.

111.author: Daryabadi
111.text: And say thou: all praise is unto Allah who hath not taken a son, and whose is no associate in the dominion, nor hath He a protector through weakness, and magnify Him with all magnificence.

111.author: Hilali & Khan
111.text: And say: "All the praises and thanks be to Allah, Who has not begotten a son (nor an offspring), and Who has no partner in (His) Dominion, nor He is low to have a Wali (helper, protector or supporter). And magnify Him with all the magnificence, [Allahu-Akbar (Allah is the Most Great)]."

111.author: Itani
111.text: And say, “Praise be to God, who has not begotten a son, nor has He a partner in sovereignty, nor has He an ally out of weakness, and glorify Him constantly.”

111.author: Maududi
111.text: And say: "All praise be to Allah Who has neither taken to Himself a son, nor has He any partner in His kingdom, nor does He need anyone, out of weakness, to protect Him." So glorify Him in a manner worthy of His glory.

111.author: Mubarakpuri
111.text: And say: "All the praises and thanks be to Allah, Who has not begotten a son, and Who has no partner in (His) dominion, nor is He low to have a supporter. And magnify Him with all magnificence."

111.author: Pickthall
111.text: And say: Praise be to Allah, Who hath not taken unto Himself a son, and Who hath no partner in the Sovereignty, nor hath He any protecting friend through dependence. And magnify Him with all magnificence.

111.author: Qarai
111.text: and say, ‘All praise belongs to Allah, who has neither taken any son, nor has He any partner in sovereignty, nor has He any wali out of weakness,’ and magnify Him with a magnification [worthy of Him].

111.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
111.text: Say: 'Praise be to Allah who has not taken a son; who has no associate in the Kingdom; nor out of humility any guardian' And exalt Him repeatedly with exaltations.

111.author: Saheeh International
111.text: And say, "Praise to Allah, who has not taken a son and has had no partner in [His] dominion and has no [need of a] protector out of weakness; and glorify Him with [great] glorification."

111.author: Sarwar
111.text: Say, "It is only God who deserves all praise. He has not begotten a son and has no partner in His Kingdom. He does not need any guardian to help Him in His need. Proclaim His greatness.

111.author: Shakir
111.text: And say: (All) praise is due to Allah, Who has not taken a son and Who has not a partner in the kingdom, and Who has not a helper to save Him from disgrace; and proclaim His greatness magnifying (Him).

111.author: Wahiduddin Khan
111.text: and say, "All praise is due to God who has never begotten a son and who has no partner in His kingdom; nor does anyone aid Him because of any weakness of His. Proclaim His greatness."

111.author: Yusuf Ali
111.text: Say: "Praise be to Allah, who begets no son, and has no partner in (His) dominion: Nor (needs) He any to protect Him from humiliation: yea, magnify Him for His greatness and glory!"

111.author: Transliteration
111.text: Waquli alhamdu lillahi allathee lam yattakhith waladan walam yakun lahu shareekun fee almulki walam yakun lahu waliyyun mina alththulli wakabbirhu takbeeran
